\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/cluster\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/env\"\n\tpm \"istio.io/istio/pkg/model\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tlog = xds.Log\n\n\t// Tracks connections, increment on each new connection.\n\tconnectionNumber = int64(0)\n)\n\n// Used only when running in KNative, to handle the load balancing behavior.\nvar firstRequest = uatomic.NewBool(true)\n\nvar knativeEnv = env.Register(\"K_REVISION\", \"\",\n\t\"KNative revision, set if running in knative\").Get()\n\n// DiscoveryStream is a server interface for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryStream = xds.DiscoveryStream\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryStream is a server interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryStream = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer\n\n// DiscoveryClient is a client interface for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n\ntype Connection struct {\n\txds.Connection\n\n\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n\t// This is included in internal events.\n\tnode *core.Node\n\n\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n\tproxy *model.Proxy\n\n\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\n\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\n\n\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\n\n\ts   *XDSServer\n\tids []string\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\n\treturn &conn.Connection\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\n// Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\ntype Event struct {\n\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\n\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n\tdone func()\n}\n\nfunc newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n\treturn &Connection{\n\t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Close() {\n\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n\tpushEv.done()\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// KnownOrderedTypeUrls has typeUrls for which we know the order of push.\nvar KnownOrderedTypeUrls = sets.New(PushOrder...)\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) adsClientCount() int {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *XDSServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t} else {\n\t\ttotalService := len(req.Push.GetAllServices())\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Pushing Services:%d ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ttotalService, s.adsClientCount(), req.Push.PushVersion)\n\t\tmonServices.Record(float64(totalService))\n\n\t\t// Make sure the ConfigsUpdated map exists\n\t\tif req.ConfigsUpdated == nil {\n\t\t\treq.ConfigsUpdated = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) addCon(conID string, con *Connection) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\ts.adsClients[conID] = con\n\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, 1)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) removeCon(conID string) {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.Lock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif con, exist := s.adsClients[conID]; !exist {\n\t\tlog.Errorf(\"ADS: Removing connection for non-existing node:%v.\", conID)\n\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t} else {\n\t\tdelete(s.adsClients, conID)\n\t\trecordXDSClients(con.proxy.Metadata.IstioVersion, -1)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Clusters() []string {\n\treturn conn.proxy.Clusters()\n}\n\n// watchedResourcesByOrder returns the ordered list of\n// watched resources for the proxy, ordered in accordance with known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then add any undeclared types\n\tfor tp, res := range allWatched {\n\t\tif !KnownOrderedTypeUrls.Contains(tp) {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn for XDS.\ntype DiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_StreamAggregatedResourcesClient\n\n// DeltaDiscoveryClient is a client interface for Delta XDS.\ntype DeltaDiscoveryClient = discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesClient\n\ntype Connection struct {\n\txds.Connection\n\n\t// Original node metadata, to avoid unmarshal/marshal.\n\t// This is included in internal events.\n\tnode *core.Node\n\n\t// proxy is the client to which this connection is established.\n\tproxy *model.Proxy\n\n\t// deltaStream is used for Delta XDS. Only one of deltaStream or stream will be set\n\tdeltaStream DeltaDiscoveryStream\n\n\tdeltaReqChan chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest\n\n\ts   *XDSServer\n\tids []string\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) XdsConnection() *xds.Connection {\n\treturn &conn.Connection\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Proxy() *model.Proxy {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\n// Event represents a config or registry event that results in a push.\ntype Event struct {\n\t// pushRequest PushRequest to use for the push.\n\tpushRequest *model.PushRequest\n\n\t// function to call once a push is finished. This must be called or future changes may be blocked.\n\tdone func()\n}\n\nfunc newConnection(peerAddr string, stream DiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n\treturn &Connection{\n\t\tConnection: xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, stream),\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Initialize(node *core.Node) error {\n\treturn conn.s.initConnection(node, conn, conn.ids)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Close() {\n\tconn.s.closeConnection(conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Watcher() xds.Watcher {\n\treturn conn.proxy\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Process(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) error {\n\treturn conn.s.processRequest(req, conn)\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) Push(ev any) error {\n\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\terr := conn.s.pushConnection(conn, pushEv)\n\tpushEv.done()\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processRequest handles one discovery request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) processRequest(req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources:%d nonce:%s version:%s \", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNames), req.ResponseNonce, req.VersionInfo)\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, req)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// For now, don't let xDS piggyback debug requests start watchers.\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNames...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond, delta := xds.ShouldRespond(con.proxy, con.ID(), req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta:  delta,\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may not have been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.computeProxyState -> SetSidecarScope) due to optimizations that skip sidecar scope\n\t// computation.\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\treturn s.pushXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n}\n\n// StreamAggregatedResources implements the ADS interface.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StreamAggregatedResources(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\treturn s.Stream(stream)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) Stream(stream DiscoveryStream) error {\n\tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n\t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %s\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\tcon.ids = ids\n\tcon.s = s\n\treturn xds.Stream(con)\n}\n\n// update the node associated with the connection, after receiving a packet from envoy, also adds the connection\n// to the tracking map.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initConnection(node *core.Node, con *Connection, identities []string) error {\n\t// Setup the initial proxy metadata\n\tproxy, err := s.initProxyMetadata(node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Check if proxy cluster has an alias configured, if yes use that as cluster ID for this proxy.\n\tif alias, exists := s.ClusterAliases[proxy.Metadata.ClusterID]; exists {\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID = alias\n\t}\n\t// To ensure push context is monotonically increasing, setup LastPushContext before we addCon. This\n\t// way only new push contexts will be registered for this proxy.\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = s.globalPushContext()\n\t// First request so initialize connection id and start tracking it.\n\tcon.SetID(connectionID(proxy.ID))\n\tcon.node = node\n\tcon.proxy = proxy\n\tif proxy.IsZTunnel() && !features.EnableAmbient {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ztunnel requires PILOT_ENABLE_AMBIENT=true\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Authorize xds clients\n\tif err := s.authorize(con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\t// Register the connection. this allows pushes to be triggered for the proxy. Note: the timing of\n\t// this and initializeProxy important. While registering for pushes *after* initialization is complete seems like\n\t// a better choice, it introduces a race condition; If we complete initialization of a new push\n\t// context between initializeProxy and addCon, we would not get any pushes triggered for the new\n\t// push context, leading the proxy to have a stale state until the next full push.\n\ts.addCon(con.ID(), con)\n\t// Register that initialization is complete. This triggers to calls that it is safe to access the\n\t// proxy\n\tdefer con.MarkInitialized()\n\n\t// Complete full initialization of the proxy\n\tif err := s.initializeProxy(con); err != nil {\n\t\ts.closeConnection(con)\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) closeConnection(con *Connection) {\n\tif con.ID() == \"\" {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\ts.removeCon(con.ID())\n\ts.WorkloadEntryController.OnDisconnect(con)\n}\n\nfunc connectionID(node string) string {\n\tid := atomic.AddInt64(&connectionNumber, 1)\n\treturn node + \"-\" + strconv.FormatInt(id, 10)\n}\n\n// initProxyMetadata initializes just the basic metadata of a proxy. This is decoupled from\n// initProxyState such that we can perform authorization before attempting expensive computations to\n// fully initialize the proxy.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initProxyMetadata(node *core.Node) (*model.Proxy, error) {\n\tmeta, err := model.ParseMetadata(node.Metadata)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\tproxy, err := model.ParseServiceNodeWithMetadata(node.Id, meta)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error()).Err()\n\t}\n\t// Update the config namespace associated with this proxy\n\tproxy.ConfigNamespace = model.GetProxyConfigNamespace(proxy)\n\tproxy.XdsNode = node\n\treturn proxy, nil\n}\n\n// setTopologyLabels sets locality, cluster, network label\n// must be called after `SetWorkloadLabels` and `SetServiceTargets`.\nfunc setTopologyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) {\n\t// This is a bit un-intuitive, but pull the locality from Labels first. The service registries have the best access to\n\t// locality information, as they can read from various sources (Node on Kubernetes, for example). They will take this\n\t// information and add it to the labels. So while the proxy may not originally have these labels,\n\t// it will by the time we get here (as a result of calling this after SetWorkloadLabels).\n\tproxy.Locality = localityFromProxyLabels(proxy)\n\tif proxy.Locality == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no locality in the registry then use the one sent as part of the discovery request.\n\t\t// This is not preferable as only the connected Pilot is aware of this proxies location, but it\n\t\t// can still help provide some client-side Envoy context when load balancing based on location.\n\t\tproxy.Locality = &core.Locality{\n\t\t\tRegion:  proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetRegion(),\n\t\t\tZone:    proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetZone(),\n\t\t\tSubZone: proxy.XdsNode.Locality.GetSubZone(),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// add topology labels to proxy labels\n\tproxy.Labels = labelutil.AugmentLabels(\n\t\tproxy.Labels,\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.ClusterID,\n\t\tutil.LocalityToString(proxy.Locality),\n\t\tproxy.GetNodeName(),\n\t\tproxy.Metadata.Network,\n\t)\n}\n\nfunc localityFromProxyLabels(proxy *model.Proxy) *core.Locality {\n\tregion, f1 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyRegion]\n\tzone, f2 := proxy.Labels[labelutil.LabelTopologyZone]\n\tsubzone, f3 := proxy.Labels[label.TopologySubzone.Name]\n\tif !f1 && !f2 && !f3 {\n\t\t// If no labels set, we didn't find the locality from the service registry. We do support a (mostly undocumented/internal)\n\t\t// label to override the locality, so respect that here as well.\n\t\tlocalityLabel := pm.GetLocalityLabel(proxy.Labels)\n\t\tif localityLabel != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn util.ConvertLocality(localityLabel)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &core.Locality{\n\t\tRegion:  region,\n\t\tZone:    zone,\n\t\tSubZone: subzone,\n\t}\n}\n\n// initializeProxy completes the initialization of a proxy. It is expected to be called only after\n// initProxyMetadata.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) initializeProxy(con *Connection) error {\n\tproxy := con.proxy\n\t// this should be done before we look for service instances, but after we load metadata\n\t// TODO fix check in kubecontroller treat echo VMs like there isn't a pod\n\tif err := s.WorkloadEntryController.OnConnect(con); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\ts.computeProxyState(proxy, nil)\n\t// Discover supported IP Versions of proxy so that appropriate config can be delivered.\n\tproxy.DiscoverIPMode()\n\n\tproxy.WatchedResources = map[string]*model.WatchedResource{}\n\t// Based on node metadata and version, we can associate a different generator.\n\tif proxy.Metadata.Generator != \"\" {\n\t\tproxy.XdsResourceGenerator = s.Generators[proxy.Metadata.Generator]\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) computeProxyState(proxy *model.Proxy, request *model.PushRequest) {\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\tvar shouldResetGateway, shouldResetSidecarScope bool\n\t// 1. If request == nil(initiation phase) or request.ConfigsUpdated == nil(global push), set proxy serviceTargets.\n\t// 2. otherwise only set when svc update, this is for the case that a service may select the proxy\n\tif request == nil || request.Forced ||\n\t\tproxy.ShouldUpdateServiceTargets(request.ConfigsUpdated) {\n\t\tproxy.SetServiceTargets(s.Env.ServiceDiscovery)\n\t\t// proxy.SetGatewaysForProxy depends on the serviceTargets,\n\t\t// so when we reset serviceTargets, should reset gateway as well.\n\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t}\n\n\t// only recompute workload labels when\n\t// 1. stream established and proxy first time initialization\n\t// 2. proxy update\n\trecomputeLabels := request == nil || request.IsProxyUpdate()\n\tif recomputeLabels {\n\t\tproxy.SetWorkloadLabels(s.Env)\n\t\tsetTopologyLabels(proxy)\n\t}\n\t// Precompute the sidecar scope and merged gateways associated with this proxy.\n\t// Saves compute cycles in networking code. Though this might be redundant sometimes, we still\n\t// have to compute this because as part of a config change, a new Sidecar could become\n\t// applicable to this proxy\n\tpush := proxy.LastPushContext\n\tif request == nil {\n\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t} else {\n\t\tpush = request.Push\n\t\tif request.Forced {\n\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor conf := range request.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\t\tswitch conf.Kind {\n\t\t\tcase kind.ServiceEntry, kind.DestinationRule, kind.VirtualService, kind.Sidecar:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Gateway:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\tcase kind.Ingress:\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetSidecarScope = true\n\t\t\t\tshouldResetGateway = true\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif shouldResetSidecarScope && shouldResetGateway {\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// compute the sidecarscope for both proxy type whenever it changes.\n\tif shouldResetSidecarScope {\n\t\tproxy.SetSidecarScope(push)\n\t}\n\t// only compute gateways for \"router\" type proxy.\n\tif shouldResetGateway && proxy.Type == model.Router {\n\t\tproxy.SetGatewaysForProxy(push)\n\t}\n\tproxy.LastPushContext = push\n\tif request != nil {\n\t\tproxy.LastPushTime = request.Start\n\t}\n}\n\n// handleWorkloadHealthcheck processes HealthInformation type Url.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) handleWorkloadHealthcheck(proxy *model.Proxy, req *discovery.DiscoveryRequest) {\n\tif features.WorkloadEntryHealthChecks {\n\t\tevent := autoregistration.HealthEvent{}\n\t\tevent.Healthy = req.ErrorDetail == nil\n\t\tif !event.Healthy {\n\t\t\tevent.Message = req.ErrorDetail.Message\n\t\t}\n\t\ts.WorkloadEntryController.QueueWorkloadEntryHealth(proxy, event)\n\t}\n}\n\n// DeltaAggregatedResources is not implemented.\n// Instead, Generators may send only updates/add, with Delete indicated by an empty spec.\n// This works if both ends follow this model. For example EDS and the API generator follow this\n// pattern.\n//\n// The delta protocol changes the request, adding unsubscribe/subscribe instead of sending full\n// list of resources. On the response it adds 'removed resources' and sends changes for everything.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) DeltaAggregatedResources(stream discovery.AggregatedDiscoveryService_DeltaAggregatedResourcesServer) error {\n\treturn s.StreamDeltas(stream)\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushConnection(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// PushOrder defines the order that updates will be pushed in. Any types not listed here will be pushed in random\n// order after the types listed here\nvar PushOrder = []string{\n\tv3.ClusterType,\n\tv3.EndpointType,\n\tv3.ListenerType,\n\tv3.RouteType,\n\tv3.SecretType,\n\tv3.AddressType,\n\tv3.WorkloadType,\n\tv3.WorkloadAuthorizationType,\n}\n\n// {\n\ts.adsClientsMutex.RLock()\n\tdefer s.adsClientsMutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn len(s.adsClients)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) ProxyUpdate(clusterID cluster.ID, ip string) {\n\tvar connection *Connection\n\n\tfor _, v := range s.Clients() {\n\t\tif v.proxy.Metadata.ClusterID == clusterID && v.proxy.IPAddresses[0] == ip {\n\t\t\tconnection = v\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// It is possible that the envoy has not connected to this pilot, maybe connected to another pilot\n\tif connection == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(connection, &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tStart:  time.Now(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyUpdate),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, with a full push.\n// Mainly used in Debug interface.\nfunc AdsPushAll(s *XDSServer) {\n\ts.AdsPushAll(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   s.globalPushContext(),\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DebugTrigger),\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t})\n}\n\n// AdsPushAll will send updates to all nodes, for a full config or incremental EDS.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) AdsPushAll(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\tlog.Infof(\"XDS: Incremental Pushing ConnectedEndpoints:%d Version:%s\",\n\t\t\ts.adsClientCount(), = make(sets.Set[model.ConfigKey])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\ts.StartPush(req)\n}\n\n// Send a signal to all connections, with a push event.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) StartPush(req *model.PushRequest) {\n\t// Push config changes, iterating over connected envoys.\n\tif log.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\tcurrentlyPending := s.pushQueue.Pending()\n\t\tif currentlyPending != 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"Starting new push while %v were still pending\", currentlyPending)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treq.Start = time.Now()\n\tfor _, p := range s.AllClients() {\n\t\ts.pushQueue.Enqueue(p, req)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) addCon(conID known push order.\nfunc (conn *Connection) watchedResourcesByOrder() []*model.WatchedResource {\n\tallWatched := conn.proxy.ShallowCloneWatchedResources()\n\tordered := make([]*model.WatchedResource, 0, len(allWatched))\n\t// first add all known types, in order\n\tfor _, tp := range PushOrder {\n\t\tif allWatched[tp] != nil {\n\t\t\tordered = append(ordered, allWatched[tp])\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// Then instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n// the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n) {\n\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n\t// Update the endpoint shards\n\tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\n\tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\n\t\t// Trigger a push\n\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\t\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\n\t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\n\t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n// This is the main method implementing EDS.\n// It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n// the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n//\n// Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\nfunc (s *XDSServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n) {\n\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n\t// Update the endpoint shards\n\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\n\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\n}\n\n// EdsGenerator implements the new Generate method for EDS, using the in-memory, optimized endpoint\n// storage in XDSServer.\ntype EdsGenerator struct {\n\tCache         model.XdsCache\n\tEndpointIndex *model.EndpointIndex\n}\n\nvar _ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &EdsGenerator{}\n\n// Map of all configs that do not impact EDS\nvar skippedEdsConfigs = sets.New(\n\tkind.Gateway,\n\tkind.VirtualService,\n\tkind.WorkloadGroup,\n\tkind.AuthorizationPolicy,\n\tkind.RequestAuthentication,\n\tkind.Secret,\n\tkind.Telemetry,\n\tkind.WasmPlugin,\n\tkind.ProxyConfig,\n\tkind.DNSName,\n)\n\nfunc edsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) bool {\n\tif res, ok := xdsNeedsPush(req, proxy); ok {\n\t\treturn res\n\t}\n\tfor config := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tif !skippedEdsConfigs.Contains(config.Kind) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\nfunc (eds *EdsGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n\tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\n\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n\t}\n\tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n\treturn resources, logDetails, nil\n}\n\nfunc (eds *EdsGenerator) GenerateDeltas(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n\tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\n\t}\n\tif !shouldUseDeltaEds(req) {\n\t\tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n\t\treturn resources, nil, logDetails, false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tresources, removed, logs := eds.buildDeltaEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n\treturn resources, removed, logs, true, nil\n}\n\nfunc shouldUseDeltaEds(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn canSendPartialFullPushes(req)\n}\n\n// canSendPartialFullPushes checks if a request contains *only* endpoints updates except `skippedEdsConfigs`.\n// This allows us to perform more efficient pushes where we only update the endpoints that did change.\nfunc canSendPartialFullPushes(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n\t// If we don't know what configs are updated, just send a full push\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor cfg := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tif skippedEdsConfigs.Contains(cfg.Kind) {\n\t\t\t// the updated config does not impact EDS, skip it\n\t\t\t// this happens when push requests are merged due to debounce\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif cfg.Kind != kind.ServiceEntry {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n\nfunc (eds *EdsGenerator) buildEndpoints(proxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails) {\n\tvar edsUpdatedServices map[string]struct{}\n\t// canSendPartialFullPushes determines if we can send a partial push (ie a subset of known CLAs).\n\t// This is safe when only Services has changed, as this implies that only the CLAs for the\n\t// associated Service changed. Note when a multi-network Service changes it triggers a push with\n\t// ConfigsUpdated=ALL, so in this case we would not enable a partial push.\n\t// Despite this code existing on the SotW code path, sending these partial pushes is still allowed;\n\t// see https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#grouping-resources-into-responses\n\tif !req.Full || canSendPartialFullPushes(req) {\n\t\tedsUpdatedServices = model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\n\t}\n\tvar resources model.Resources\n\tempty := 0\n\tcached := 0\n\tregenerated := 0\n\tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\n\t\tif edsUpdatedServices != nil {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t// Cluster was not updated, skip recomputing. This happens when we get an incremental update for a\n\t\t\t\t// specific Hostname. On connect or for full push edsUpdatedServices will be empty.\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(clusterName, proxy, req.Push)\n\n\t\t// We skip cache if assertions are enabled, so that the cache will assert our eviction logic is correct\n\t\tif !features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\t\tcachedEndpoint := eds.Cache.Get(&builder)\n\t\t\tif cachedEndpoint != nil {\n\t\t\t\tresources = append(resources, cachedEndpoint)\n\t\t\t\tcached++\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// generate eds from beginning\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tl := builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(eds.EndpointIndex)\n\t\t\tif l == nil {\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tregenerated++\n\n\t\t\tif len(l.Endpoints) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tempty++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresource := &discovery.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tName:     l.ClusterName,\n\t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(l),\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresources = append(resources, resource)\n\t\t\teds.Cache.Add(&builder, req, resource)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn resources, model.XdsLogDetails{\n\t\tIncremental:    len(edsUpdatedServices) != 0,\n\t\tAdditionalInfo: fmt.Sprintf(\"empty:%v cached:%v/%v\", empty, cached, cached+regenerated),\n\t}\n}\n\n// TODO(@hzxuzhonghu): merge with buildEndpoints\nfunc (eds *EdsGenerator) buildDeltaEndpoints(proxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, []string, model.XdsLogDetails) {\n\tedsUpdatedServices := model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\n\tvar resources model.Resources\n\tvar removed []string\n\tempty := 0\n\tcached := 0\n\tregenerated := 0\n\n\tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\n\t\t// filter out eds that are not updated for clusters\n\t\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tbuilder := endpoints.NewEndpointBuilder(clusterName, proxy, req.Push)\n\t\t// if a service is not found, it means the cluster is removed\n\t\tif !builder.ServiceFound() {\n\t\t\tremoved = append(removed, clusterName)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// We skip cache if assertions are enabled, so that the cache will assert our eviction logic is correct\n\t\tif !features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\t\tcachedEndpoint := eds.Cache.Get(&builder)\n\t\t\tif cachedEndpoint != nil {\n\t\t\t\tresources = append(resources, cachedEndpoint)\n\t\t\t\tcached++\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\t// generate new eds cache\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tl := builder.BuildClusterLoadAssignment(eds.EndpointIndex)\n\t\t\tif l == nil {\n\t\t\t\tremoved = append(removed, clusterName)\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tregenerated++\n\t\t\tif len(l.Endpoints) == 0 {\n\t\t\t\tempty++\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresource := &discovery.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tName:     l.ClusterName,\n\t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(l),\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresources = append(resources, resource)\n\t\t\teds.Cache.Add(&builder, req, resource)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn resources, removed, model.XdsLogDetails{\n\t\tIncremental:    len(edsUpdatedServices) != 0,\n\t\tAdditionalInfo: fmt.Sprintf(\"empty:%v cached:%v/%v\", empty, cached, cached+regenerated),\n\t}\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1055,"cache_read_input_tokens":88683,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1055},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"731dac43-d059-4b49-b6ef-e931483c341b","uuid":"d6a7fecb-f4bf-4b5b-9ac3-b3f13ef85d37"}

And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) EDSUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n) {\n\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n\t// Update the endpoint shards\n\tpushType := s.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, true)\n\tif pushType == model.IncrementalPush || pushType == model.FullPush {\n\t\t// Trigger a push\n\t\ts.ConfigUpdate(&model.PushRequest{\n\t\t\tFull:           pushType == model.FullPush,\n\t\t\tConfigsUpdated: sets.New(model.ConfigKey{Kind: kind.ServiceEntry, Name: serviceName, Namespace: namespace}),\n\t\t\tReason:         model.NewReasonStats(model.EndpointUpdate),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// EDSCacheUpdate computes destination address membership across all clusters and networks.\n// This is the main method implementing EDS.\n// It replaces InstancesByPort in model - instead of iterating over all endpoints it uses\n// the hostname-keyed map. And it avoids the conversion from Endpoint to ServiceEntry to envoy\n// on each step: instead the conversion happens once, when an endpoint is first discovered.\n//\n// Note: the difference with `EDSUpdate` is that it only update the cache rather than requesting a push\nfunc (s *XDSServer) EDSCacheUpdate(shard model.ShardKey, serviceName string, namespace string,\n\tistioEndpoints []*model.IstioEndpoint,\n) {\n\tinboundEDSUpdates.Increment()\n\t// Update the endpoint shards\n\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.UpdateServiceEndpoints(shard, serviceName, namespace, istioEndpoints, false)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) RemoveShard(shardKey model.ShardKey) {\n\ts.Env.EndpointIndex.DeleteShard(shardKey)\n}\n\n// EdsGenerator implements the new Generate method for EDS, using the in-memory, optimized endpoint\n// storage in XDSServer.\ntype EdsGenerator struct {\n\tCache         model.XdsCache\n\tEndpointIndex *model.EndpointIndex\n}\n\nvar _ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &EdsGenerator{}\n\n// Map of all configs that do not impact EDS\nvar skippedEdsConfigs = edsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) bool {\n\tif res, ok := xdsNeedsPush(req, proxy); ok {\n\t\treturn res\n\t}\n\tfor config := range req.ConfigsUpdated {\n\t\tif (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n\tif !edsNeedsPush(req, proxy) {\n\t\treturn nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, nil\n\t}\n\tresources, logDetails := eds.buildEndpoints(proxy, req, w)\n\treturn resources, logDetails, nil\n}\n\nfunc (eds *EdsGenerator) GenerateDeltas(proxy *model.Proxy, req *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, w)\n\treturn resources, removed, logs, true, nil\n}\n\nfunc shouldUseDeltaEds(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n\tif !req.Full {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\treturn canSendPartialFullPushes(req)\n}\n\n// canSendPartialFullPushes checks if a request contains *only* endpoints updates except `skippedEdsConfigs`.\n// This allows us to perform more efficient pushes where we only update the endpoints that did change.\nfunc canSendPartialFullPushes(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n\t// If we don't know what configs are updated, just send a full push\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tfor cfg := partial push (ie a subset of known CLAs).\n\t// This is safe when only Services has changed, as this implies that only the CLAs for the\n\t// associated Service changed. Note when a multi-network Service changes it triggers a push with\n\t// ConfigsUpdated=ALL, so in this case we would not enable a partial push.\n\t// Despite this code existing on the SotW code path, sending these partial pushes is still allowed;\n\t// see https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#grouping-resources-into-responses\n\tif !req.Full || canSendPartialFullPushes(req) {\n\t\tedsUpdatedServices = model.ConfigNamesOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.ServiceEntry)\n\t}\n\tvar resources model.Resources\n\tempty := 0\n\tcached := 0\n\tregenerated := 0\n\tfor clusterName := range w.ResourceNames {\n\t\tif edsUpdatedServices != nil {\n\t\t\tif _, ok := edsUpdatedServices[model.ParseSubsetKeyHostname(clusterName)]; !ok {\n\t\t\t\t// Cluster was not updated, skip recomputing. (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\\n44: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n45: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n46: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n47: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n48: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n49: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n50: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n51: \\t}\\n52: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n53: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n54: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n55: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n56: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n57: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n58: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n59: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n60: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n61: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\n64: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n65: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n66: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n67: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n68: \\t}\\n69: \\n70: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n71: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n72: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n73: \\t}\\n74: \\n75: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n76: \\tif err != nil {\\n77: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\tif ids != nil {\\n80: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n81: \\t} else {\\n82: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %v\\\", peerAddr)\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\n85: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n86: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n87: \\tcon := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected\\n90: \\t// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions\\n91: \\t// with push. According to the spec: \\\"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\\n92: \\t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\\\"\\n93: \\n94: \\t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\\n95: \\t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\\n96: \\tgo s.receiveDelta(con, ids)\\n97: \\n98: \\t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because\\n99: \\t// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout\\n100: \\t// here. Note: if we have a huge\\n124: \\t\\t// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;\\n125: \\t\\t// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests\\n126: \\t\\t// cannot completely starve pushes. err != nil {\\n170: \\t\\t\\treturn err\\n171: \\t\\t}\\n172: \\t}\\n173: \\n174: \\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\\n175: \\treturn nil\\n176: }\\n177: \\n178: func (s *DiscoveryServer) receiveDelta(con *Connection, identities []string) {\\n179: \\tdefer func() {\\n180: \\t\\tclose(con.deltaReqChan)\\n181: \\t\\tclose(con.ErrorCh())\\n182: \\t\\t// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream\\n183: \\t\\tselect {\\n184: \\t\\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\\n185: \\t\\tdefault:\\n186: \\t\\t\\tclose(con.InitializedCh())\\n187: \\t\\t}\\n188: \\t}()\\n189: \\tfirstRequest := true\\n190: \\tfor {\\n191: \\t\\treq, err := con.deltaStream.Recv()\\n192: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n193: \\t\\t\\tif istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {\\n194: \\t\\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n195: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n196: \\t\\t\\t}\\n197: \\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\\n198: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID(), err)\\n199: \\t\\t\\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\\n200: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n201: \\t\\t}\\n202: \\t\\t// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.\\n203: \\t\\tif firstRequest {\\n204: \\t\\t\\t// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request\\n205: \\t\\t\\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n208: \\t\\t\\t}\\n209: \\t\\t\\tfirstRequest = false\\n210: \\t\\t\\tif req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == \\\"\\\" {\\n211: \\t\\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, \\\"missing node information\\\").Err()\\n212: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n213: \\t\\t\\t}\\n214: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {\\n215: \\t\\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n217: \\t\\t\\t}\\n218: \\t\\t\\tdefer s.closeConnection(con)\\n219: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: new delta connection for node:%s\\\", con.ID())\\n220: \\t\\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\t\\tselect {\\n223: \\t\\tcase con.deltaReqChan <- req:\\n224: \\t\\tcase <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():\\n225: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n226: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n227: \\t\\t}\\n228: \\t}\\n229: }\\n230: \\n231: func (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {\\n232: \\tsendResonse := func() error {\\n233: \\t\\tstart := time.Now()\\n234: \\t\\tdefer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()\\n235: \\t\\treturn conn.deltaStream.Send(res)\\n236: \\t}\\n237: \\terr := sendResonse()\\n238: \\tif err == nil {\\n239: \\t\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n240: \\t\\t\\tconn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n241: \\t\\t\\t\\tif wr == nil {\\n242: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}\\n243: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\t// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though\\n245: \\t\\t\\t\\tif newResourceNames != nil {\\n246: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n248: \\t\\t\\t\\twr.NonceSent = res.Nonce\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\twr.LastSendTime = time.Now()\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn wr\\n254: \\t\\t\\t})\\n255: \\t\\t}\\n256: \\t} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\\n257: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"Timeout writing %s: %v\\\", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))\\n258: \\t\\txds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\treturn err\\n261: }\\n262: \\n263: // processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n264: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n265: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n266: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n267: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n268: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s\\\", stype,\\n269: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)\\n270: \\n271: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n272: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))\\n273: \\t\\treturn nil\\n274: \\t}\\n275: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n276: \\t\\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con,\\n277: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},\\n278: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n279: \\t}\\n280: \\n281: \\tshouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)\\n282: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n283: \\t\\treturn nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\tsubs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)\\n287: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n288: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n289: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n290: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n291: \\n292: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n293: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n294: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n295: \\t\\tStart: con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n296: \\t\\tDelta: model.ResourceDelta{\\n297: \\t\\t\\t// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info\\n298: \\t\\t\\tSubscribed:   subs,\\n299: \\t\\t\\tUnsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete(\\\"*\\\"),\\n300: \\t\\t},\\n301: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n302: \\t}\\n303: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.\\n304: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n305: \\t// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).\\n306: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n307: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\n310: \\terr := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n311: \\tif err != nil {\\n312: \\t\\treturn err\\n313: \\t}\\n314: \\t// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.\\n315: \\t// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if\\n316: \\t// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).\\n317: \\t// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,\\n318: \\t// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed\\n319: \\t// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues\\n320: \\t// with EDS and SDS.\\n321: \\t// This can happen with the following sequence\\n322: \\t// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.\\n323: \\t// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.\\n324: \\t// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.\\n325: \\t// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends\\n326: \\t//    an EDS request for them.\\n327: \\t// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response\\n328: \\t// \\t  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.\\n329: \\t// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.\\n330: \\tif req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {\\n331: \\t\\treturn nil\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\treturn s.forceEDSPush(con)\\n334: }\\n335: \\n336: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {\\n337: \\tif dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {\\n338: \\t\\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n339: \\t\\t\\tFull:   true,\\n340: \\t\\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n341: \\t\\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),\\n342: \\t\\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n343: \\t\\t\\tForced: true,\\n344: \\t\\t}\\n345: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.\\\", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())\\n346: \\t\\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\treturn nil\\n349: }\\n350: \\n351: // shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol\\n352: // using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.\\n353: func shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {\\n354: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)\\n355: \\n356: \\t// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.\\n357: \\t// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info\\n358: \\t// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.\\n359: \\tif request.ErrorDetail != nil {\\n360: \\t\\terrCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)\\n361: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s\\\", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())\\n362: \\t\\txds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())\\n363: \\t\\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n364: \\t\\t\\twr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn wr\\n366: \\t\\t})\\n367: \\t\\treturn false\\n368: \\t}\\n369: \\n370: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v\\\", stype, con.ID(),\\n371: \\t\\trequest.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)\\n372: \\tpreviousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)\\n373: \\n374: \\t// This can happen in two cases:\\n375: \\t// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod\\n376: \\t// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have\\n377: \\t// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either\\n378: \\t// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.\\n379: \\t// We should always respond with the current resource names.\\n380: \\tif previousInfo == nil {\\n381: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Lock()\\n382: \\t\\tdefer con.proxy.Unlock()\\n383: \\n384: \\t\\tif len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {\\n385: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, len(request.InitialResourceVersions))\\n386: \\t\\t} else {\\n387: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: INIT %s %s\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\\n388: \\t\\t}\\n389: \\n390: \\t\\tres, wildcard, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, request)\\n391: \\t\\tskip := request.TypeUrl == v3.AddressType && wildcard\\n392: \\t\\tif skip {\\n393: \\t\\t\\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we do not store ResourceName.\\n394: \\t\\t\\t// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.\\n395: \\t\\t\\tres = nil\\n396: \\t\\t}\\n397: \\t\\tcon.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{\\n398: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\\n399: \\t\\t\\tResourceNames: res,\\n400: \\t\\t\\tWildcard:      wildcard,\\n401: \\t\\t}\\n402: \\t\\treturn true\\n403: \\t}\\n404: \\n405: \\t// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.\\n406: \\t// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.\\n407: \\tif request.ResponseNonce != \\\"\\\" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {\\n408: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s\\\", stype,\\n409: \\t\\t\\tcon.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)\\n410: \\t\\txds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()\\n411: \\t\\treturn false\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\n414: \\t// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.\\n415: \\t// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.\\n416: \\t// In this case response_nonce is empty.\\n417: \\tspontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == \\\"\\\"\\n418: \\n419: \\tvar alwaysRespond bool\\n420: \\tvar subChanged bool\\n421: \\n422: \\t// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.\\n423: \\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n424: \\t\\twr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)\\n425: \\t\\tif !spontaneousReq {\\n426: \\t\\t\\t// Clear last error, we got an ACK.\\n427: \\t\\t\\t// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info in place.\\n428: \\t\\t\\twr.LastError = \\\"\\\"\\n429: \\t\\t\\twr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce\\n430: \\t\\t}\\n431: \\t\\talwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond\\n432: \\t\\twr.AlwaysRespond = false\\n433: \\t\\treturn wr\\n434: \\t})\\n435: \\n436: \\t// It is invalid in the below two cases:\\n437: \\t// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.\\n438: \\t// 2. subscribed resources changes from ACK.\\n439: \\tif spontaneousReq && !subChanged || !spontaneousReq && subChanged {\\n440: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\\\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\\\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)\\n441: \\t\\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n442: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\\\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))\\n443: \\t\\t}\\n444: \\t}\\n445: \\n446: \\t// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce\\n447: \\t// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.\\n448: \\tif !subChanged {\\n449: \\t\\t// We should always respond \\\"alwaysRespond\\\" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming\\n450: \\t\\t// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.\\n451: \\t\\tif alwaysRespond {\\n452: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.\\\", stype, con.ID())\\n453: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n454: \\t\\t}\\n455: \\n456: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: ACK %s %s\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\\n457: \\t\\treturn false\\n458: \\t}\\n459: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE %s %s\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\\n460: \\n461: \\treturn true\\n462: }\\n463: \\n464: // Push a Delta XDS resource for the given connection.\\n465: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushDeltaXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\\n466: \\tif w == nil {\\n467: \\t\\treturn nil\\n468: \\t}\\n469: \\tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\\n470: \\tif gen == nil {\\n471: \\t\\treturn nil\\n472: \\t}\\n473: \\tt0 := time.Now()\\n474: \\n475: \\toriginalW := w\\n476: \\t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\\n477: \\t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\\n478: \\t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\\n479: \\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\\n480: \\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\\n481: \\tvar logFiltered string\\n482: \\tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {\\n483: \\t\\t// Some types opt out of this and natively handle shouldSetWatchedResources(w) {\\n530: \\t\\t// Set the new watched resources. Do not write to w directly, as it can be a copy from the 'filtered' logic above\\n531: \\t\\tif usedDelta {\\n532: \\t\\t\\t// Apply the delta\\n533: \\t\\t\\tnewResourceNames = w.ResourceNames.Copy().\\n534: \\t\\t\\t\\tDeleteAll(resp.RemovedResources...)\\n535: \\t\\t\\tfor _, r := range res {\\n536: \\t\\t\\t\\tnewResourceNames.Insert(r.Name)\\n537: \\t\\t\\t}\\n538: \\t\\t} else {\\n539: \\t\\t\\tnewResourceNames = resourceNamesSet(res)\\n540: \\t\\t}\\n541: \\t}\\n542: \\tif neverRemoveDelta(w.TypeUrl) {\\n543: \\t\\tresp.RemovedResources = nil\\n544: \\n549: \\tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\\n550: \\tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\\n551: \\n552: \\tptype := \\\"PUSH\\\"\\n553: \\tinfo := {\\n565: \\t\\tlogger := deltaLog.Debugf\\n566: \\t\\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\\n567: \\t\\t\\tlogger = deltaLog.Warnf\\n568: \\t\\t}\\n569: \\t\\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\\n570: \\n596: func resourceNamesSet(res model.Resources) sets.Set[string] {\\n597: \\treturn sets.New(slices.Map(res, func(r *discovery.Resource) string {\\n598: \\t\\treturn r.Name\\n599: \\t})...)\\n600: }\\n601: \\n602: // requiresResourceNamesModification checks if a generator needs mutable access to w.ResourceNames.\\n603: // This is used when resources are spontaneously pushed during Delta XDS\\n604: func requiresResourceNamesModification(url string) bool {\\n605: \\treturn url == v3.AddressType || url == v3.WorkloadType\\n606: }\\n607: \\n608: // neverRemoveDelta checks if a type should never remove resources\\n609: func neverRemoveDelta(url string) bool {\\n610: \\t// https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/32823\\n611: \\t// We want to garbage collect extensions when they are no longer referenced, rather than delete immediately\\n612: \\treturn url == v3.ExtensionConfigurationType\\n613: }\\n614: \\n615: // shouldSetWatchedResources indicates whether we should set the watched resources for a given type.\\n616: // for some type like `Address` we customly handle it in the generator\\n617: func shouldSetWatchedResources(w *model.WatchedResource) bool {\\n618: \\tif xds.IsWildcardTypeURL(w.TypeUrl)\\n624: }\\n625: \\n626: func newDeltaConnection(peerAddr string, stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) *Connection {\\n627: \\treturn &Connection{\\n628: \\t\\tConnection:   xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, nil),\\n629: \\t\\tdeltaStream:  stream,\\n630: \\t\\tdeltaReqChan: make(chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, 1),\\n631: \\t}\\n632: }\\n633: \\n634: // To satisfy methods that need DiscoveryRequest.     request.Node,\\n638: \\t\\tResourceNames: request.ResourceNamesSubscribe,\\n639: \\t\\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\\n640: \\t\\tResponseNonce: request.ResponseNonce,\\n641: \\t\\tErrorDetail:
