\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\\\"\\n31: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\\\"\\n32: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\\\"\\n33: \\tv3 \\\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\\\"\\n34: \\tistiolog \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/log\\\"\\n35: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\\\"\\n36: \\t_ \\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/protomarshal\\\" // Ensure we get the more efficient vtproto gRPC encoder\\n37: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\\\"\\n38: \\t\\\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\\\"\\n39: )\\n40: \\n41: var deltaLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\\\"delta\\\", \\\"delta xds debugging\\\")\\n42: \\n43: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\\n44: \\tif knativeEnv != \\\"\\\" && firstRequest.Load() {\\n45: \\t\\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \\\"loading\\\" request. During\\n46: \\t\\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\\n47: \\t\\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\\n48: \\t\\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\\n49: \\t\\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\\n50: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \\\"server warmup not complete; try again\\\")\\n51: \\t}\\n52: \\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\\n53: \\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\\n54: \\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\\n55: \\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\\n56: \\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\\n57: \\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\\n58: \\t// ip tables update latencies.\\n59: \\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\\n60: \\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\\n61: \\t\\treturn errors.New(\\\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\\\")\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\n64: \\tctx := stream.Context()\\n65: \\tpeerAddr := \\\"0.0.0.0\\\"\\n66: \\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\\n67: \\t\\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\\n68: \\t}\\n69: \\n70: \\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\\n71: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\\\", peerAddr, err)\\n72: \\t\\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \\\"request rate limit exceeded: %v\\\", err)\\n73: \\t}\\n74: \\n75: \\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\\n76: \\tif err != nil {\\n77: \\t\\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\tif ids != nil {\\n80: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\\\", peerAddr, ids)\\n81: \\t} else {\\n82: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Unauthenticated XDS: %v\\\", peerAddr)\\n83: \\t}\\n84: \\n85: \\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\\n86: \\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\\n87: \\tcon := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)\\n88: \\n89: \\t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected\\n90: \\t// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions\\n91: \\t// with push. According to the spec: \\\"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\\n92: \\t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\\\"\\n93: \\n94: \\t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\\n95: \\t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\\n96: \\tgo s.receiveDelta(con, ids)\\n97: \\n98: \\t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because\\n99: \\t// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout\\n100: \\t// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to\\n101: \\t// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the\\n102: \\t// initialization is complete.\\n103: \\t<-con.InitializedCh()\\n104: \\n105: \\tfor {\\n106: \\t\\t// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.\\n107: \\t\\t// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)\\n108: \\t\\t// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.\\n109: \\t\\tselect {\\n110: \\t\\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\\n111: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n112: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\\n113: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n114: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n115: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n116: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\\n117: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\\n118: \\t\\t\\t}\\n119: \\t\\tcase <-con.StopCh():\\n120: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n121: \\t\\tdefault:\\n122: \\t\\t}\\n123: \\t\\t// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge\\n124: \\t\\t// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;\\n125: \\t\\t// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests\\n126: \\t\\t// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.\\n127: \\t\\tselect {\\n128: \\t\\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\\n129: \\t\\t\\tif ok {\\n130: \\t\\t\\t\\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\\n131: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n133: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\\n135: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\\n136: \\t\\t\\t}\\n137: \\t\\tcase ev := <-con.PushCh():\\n138: \\t\\t\\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\\n139: \\t\\t\\terr := s.pushConnectionDelta(con, pushEv)\\n140: \\t\\t\\tpushEv.done()\\n141: \\t\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n142: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn err\\n143: \\t\\t\\t}\\n144: \\t\\tcase <-con.StopCh():\\n145: \\t\\t\\treturn nil\\n146: \\t\\t}\\n147: \\t}\\n148: }\\n149: \\n150: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\\n151: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnectionDelta(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\\n152: \\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\\n153: \\n154: \\tif pushRequest.Full {\\n155: \\t\\t// Update Proxy with current information.\\n156: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\n159: \\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\\n160: \\tif !needsPush {\\n161: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\\\", con.ID())\\n162: \\t\\treturn nil\\n163: \\t}\\n164: \\n165: \\t// Send pushes to all generators\\n166: \\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\\n167: \\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\\n168: \\tfor _, w := range wrl {\\n169: \\t\\tif err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, w, pushRequest); The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.\\n203: \\t\\tif firstRequest {\\n204: \\t\\t\\t// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request\\n205: \\t\\t\\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\tlog.Warnf(\\\"ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n208: \\t\\t\\t}\\n209: \\t\\t\\tfirstRequest = false\\n210: \\t\\t\\tif req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == \\\"\\\" {\\n211: \\t\\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, \\\"missing node information\\\").Err()\\n212: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n213: \\t\\t\\t}\\n214: \\t\\t\\tif err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {\\n215: \\t\\t\\t\\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n217: \\t\\t\\t}\\n218: \\t\\t\\tdefer s.closeConnection(con)\\n219: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: new delta connection for node:%s\\\", con.ID())\\n220: \\t\\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\t\\tselect {\\n223: \\t\\tcase con.deltaReqChan <- req:\\n224: \\t\\tcase <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():\\n225: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed\\\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\\n226: \\t\\t\\treturn\\n227: \\t\\t}\\n228: \\t}\\n229: }\\n230: \\n231: func (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {\\n232: \\tsendResonse := func() error {\\n233: \\t\\tstart := time.Now()\\n234: \\t\\tdefer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()\\n235: \\t\\treturn conn.deltaStream.Send(res)\\n236: \\t}\\n237: \\terr := sendResonse()\\n238: \\tif err == nil {\\n239: \\t\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n240: \\t\\t\\tconn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n241: \\t\\t\\t\\tif wr == nil {\\n242: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}\\n243: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n244: \\t\\t\\t\\t// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though\\n245: \\t\\t\\t\\tif newResourceNames != nil {\\n246: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames\\n247: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n248: \\t\\t\\t\\twr.NonceSent = res.Nonce\\n249: \\t\\t\\t\\twr.LastSendTime = time.Now()\\n250: \\t\\t\\t\\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\\n251: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\twr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)\\n252: \\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n253: \\t\\t\\t\\treturn wr\\n254: \\t\\t\\t})\\n255: \\t\\t}\\n256: \\t} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\\n257: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"Timeout writing %s: %v\\\", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))\\n258: \\t\\txds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()\\n259: \\t}\\n260: \\treturn err\\n261: }\\n262: \\n263: // processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\\n264: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\\n265: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\\n266: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\\n267: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\\n268: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s\\\", stype,\\n269: \\t\\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)\\n270: \\n271: \\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\\n272: \\t\\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))\\n273: \\t\\treturn nil\\n274: \\t}\\n275: \\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\\n276: \\t\\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con,\\n277: \\t\\t\\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},\\n278: \\t\\t\\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\\n279: \\t}\\n280: \\n281: \\tshouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)\\n282: \\tif !shouldRespond {\\n283: \\t\\treturn nil\\n284: \\t}\\n285: \\n286: \\tsubs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)\\n287: \\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n288: \\t\\tFull:   true,\\n289: \\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n290: \\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\\n291: \\n292: \\t\\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\\n293: \\t\\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\\n294: \\t\\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\\n295: \\t\\tStart: con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n296: \\t\\tDelta: model.ResourceDelta{\\n297: \\t\\t\\t// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info\\n298: \\t\\t\\tSubscribed:   subs,\\n299: \\t\\t\\tUnsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete(\\\"*\\\"),\\n300: \\t\\t},\\n301: \\t\\tForced: true,\\n302: \\t}\\n303: \\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.\\n304: \\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\\n305: \\t// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).\\n306: \\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\\n307: \\t\\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\\n308: \\t}\\n309: \\n310: \\terr := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\\n311: \\tif err != nil {\\n312: \\t\\treturn err\\n313: \\t}\\n314: \\t// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.\\n315: \\t// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if\\n316: \\t// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).\\n317: \\t// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,\\n318: \\t// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed\\n319: \\t// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues\\n320: \\t// with EDS and SDS.\\n321: \\t// This can happen with the following sequence\\n322: \\t// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.\\n323: \\t// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.\\n324: \\t// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.\\n325: \\t// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends\\n326: \\t//    an EDS request for them.\\n327: \\t// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response\\n328: \\t// \\t  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.\\n329: \\t// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.\\n330: \\tif req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {\\n331: \\t\\treturn nil\\n332: \\t}\\n333: \\treturn s.forceEDSPush(con)\\n334: }\\n335: \\n336: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {\\n337: \\tif dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {\\n338: \\t\\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\\n339: \\t\\t\\tFull:   true,\\n340: \\t\\t\\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\\n341: \\t\\t\\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),\\n342: \\t\\t\\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\\n343: \\t\\t\\tForced: true,\\n344: \\t\\t}\\n345: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.\\\", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())\\n346: \\t\\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)\\n347: \\t}\\n348: \\treturn nil\\n349: }\\n350: \\n351: // shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol\\n352: // using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.\\n353: func shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {\\n354: \\tstype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)\\n355: \\n356: \\t// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.\\n357: \\t// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info\\n358: \\t// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.\\n359: \\tif request.ErrorDetail != nil {\\n360: \\t\\terrCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)\\n361: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\\\"ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s\\\", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())\\n362: \\t\\txds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())\\n363: \\t\\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n364: \\t\\t\\twr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()\\n365: \\t\\t\\treturn wr\\n366: \\t\\t})\\n367: \\t\\treturn false\\n368: \\t}\\n369: \\n370: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v\\\", stype, con.ID(),\\n371: \\t\\trequest.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)\\n372: \\tpreviousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)\\n373: \\n374: \\t// This can happen in two cases:\\n375: \\t// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod\\n376: \\t// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have\\n377: \\t// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either\\n378: \\t// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.\\n379: \\t// We should always respond with the current resource names.\\n380: \\tif previousInfo == nil {\\n381: \\t\\tcon.proxy.Lock()\\n382: \\t\\tdefer con.proxy.Unlock()\\n383: \\n384: \\t\\tif len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {\\n385: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, len(request.InitialResourceVersions))\\n386: \\t\\t} else {\\n387: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: INIT %s %s\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\\n388: \\t\\t}\\n389: \\n390: \\t\\tres, wildcard, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, request)\\n391: \\t\\tskip := request.TypeUrl == v3.AddressType && wildcard\\n392: \\t\\tif skip {\\n393: \\t\\t\\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we do not store ResourceName.\\n394: \\t\\t\\t// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.\\n395: \\t\\t\\tres = nil\\n396: \\t\\t}\\n397: \\t\\tcon.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{\\n398: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\\n399: \\t\\t\\tResourceNames: res,\\n400: \\t\\t\\tWildcard:      wildcard,\\n401: \\t\\t}\\n402: \\t\\treturn true\\n403: \\t}\\n404: \\n405: \\t// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.\\n406: \\t// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.\\n407: \\tif request.ResponseNonce != \\\"\\\" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {\\n408: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s\\\", stype,\\n409: \\t\\t\\tcon.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)\\n410: \\t\\txds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()\\n411: \\t\\treturn false\\n412: \\t}\\n413: \\n414: \\t// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.\\n415: \\t// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.\\n416: \\t// In this case response_nonce is empty.\\n417: \\tspontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == \\\"\\\"\\n418: \\n419: \\tvar alwaysRespond bool\\n420: \\tvar subChanged bool\\n421: \\n422: \\t// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.\\n423: \\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\\n424: \\t\\twr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)\\n425: \\t\\tif !spontaneousReq {\\n426: \\t\\t\\t// Clear last error, we got an ACK.\\n427: \\t\\t\\t// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info false\\n433: \\t\\treturn wr\\n434: \\t})\\n435: \\n436: \\t// It is invalid in the below two cases:\\n437: \\t// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.\\n438: \\t// 2. subscribed resources changes from ACK.\\n439: \\tif spontaneousReq && !subChanged || !spontaneousReq && subChanged {\\n440: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\\\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\\\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)\\n441: \\t\\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\\n442: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\\\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))\\n443: \\t\\t}\\n444: \\t}\\n445: \\n446: \\t// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce\\n447: \\t// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.\\n448: \\tif !subChanged {\\n449: \\t\\t// We should always respond \\\"alwaysRespond\\\" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming\\n450: \\t\\t// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.\\n451: \\t\\tif alwaysRespond {\\n452: \\t\\t\\tdeltaLog.Infof(\\\"ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.\\\", stype, con.ID())\\n453: \\t\\t\\treturn true\\n454: \\t\\t}\\n455: \\n456: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: ACK %s %s\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\\n457: \\t\\treturn false\\n458: \\t}\\n459: \\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE %s %s\\\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\\n460: \\n461: \\treturn true\\n462: }\\n463: \\n464: // Push a Delta XDS resource for the given connection.\\n465: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushDeltaXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\\n466: \\tif w == nil {\\n467: \\t\\treturn nil\\n468: \\t}\\n469: \\tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\\n470: \\tif gen == nil {\\n471: \\t\\treturn nil\\n472: \\t}\\n473: \\tt0 := time.Now()\\n474: \\n475: \\toriginalW := w\\n476: \\t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\\n477: \\t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\\n478: \\t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\\n479: \\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\\n480: \\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\\n481: \\tvar logFiltered string\\n482: \\tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {\\n483: \\t\\t// Some types opt out of this and natively handle req.Delta\\n484: \\t\\tlogFiltered = \\\" filtered:\\\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\\n485: \\t\\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\\n486: \\t\\t\\tTypeUrl: logdata model.XdsLogDetails\\n494: \\tvar usedDelta bool\\n495: \\tvar err error\\n496: \\tswitch g := gen.(type) {\\n497: \\tcase model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator:\\n498: \\t\\tres, deletedRes, \\t\\t\\ts.compareDiff(con, originalW, fullRes, res, deletedRes, usedDelta, req.Delta, l.Incremental)\\n502: \\t\\t}\\n503: \\tcase model.XdsResourceGenerator:\\n504: \\t\\tres, logdata, err = \\tif usedDelta {\\n519: \\t\\tresp.RemovedResources = deletedRes\\n520: \\t} else if req.Full {\\n521: \\t\\t// similar to sotw\\n522: \\t\\tremoved := w.ResourceNames.Copy()\\n523: \\t\\tfor _, r := Do not write to w directly, as it can be a copy from the 'filtered' logic above\\n531: \\t\\tif usedDelta {\\n532: \\t\\t\\t// Apply the delta\\n533: \\t\\t\\tnewResourceNames = \\t\\t} else {\\n539: \\t\\t\\tnewResourceNames = resourceNamesSet(res)\\n540: \\t\\t}\\n541: \\t}\\n542: \\tif neverRemoveDelta(w.TypeUrl) {\\n543: \\t\\tresp.RemovedResources = nil\\n544: \\t}\\n545: \\tif len(resp.RemovedResources) > 0 {\\n546: \\t\\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\\\"ADS:%v REMOVE for node:%s %v\\\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.ID(), resp.RemovedResources)\\n547: \\t}\\n548: \\n596: func resourceNamesSet(res model.Resources) sets.Set[string] {\\n597: \\treturn sets.New(slices.Map(res, func(r *discovery.Resource) string {\\n598: \\t\\treturn r.Name\\n599: \\t})...)\\n600: are no longer referenced, rather than delete immediately\\n612: \\treturn url == v3.ExtensionConfigurationType\\n613: }\\n614: \\n615: // shouldSetWatchedResources indicates whether we should set the methods that need DiscoveryRequest. *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) (sets.String, bool, bool) {\\n647: \\tres := existing\\n648: \\tif res == nil {\\n649: \\t\\tres = sets.New[string]()\\n650: \\t}\\n651: \\tchanged := false\\n652: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/features\"\n30: \tistiogrpc \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/grpc\"\n31: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n32: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/networking/util\"\n33: \tv3 efficient vtproto gRPC encoder\n37: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n38: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/xds\"\n39: )\n40: \n41: var deltaLog = istiolog.RegisterScope(\"delta\", \"delta xds debugging\")\n42: \n43: func (s *DiscoveryServer) StreamDeltas(stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) error {\n44: \tif knativeEnv != \"\" && firstRequest.Load() {\n45: \t\t// How scaling works in knative is the first request is the \"loading\" request. During\n46: \t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n47: \t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n48: \t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n49: \t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n50: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n51: \t}\n52: \t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n53: \t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n54: \t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n55: \t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n56: \t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n57: \t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n58: \t// ip tables update latencies.\n59: \t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n60: \tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n61: \t\treturn errors.New(\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n62: \t}\n63: \n64: \tctx := stream.Context()\n65: \tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n66: \tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n67: \t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n68: \t}\n69: \n70: \tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n71: \t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n72: \t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n73: \t}\n74: \n75: \tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n76: \tif err != nil {\n77: \t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n78: \t}\n79: \tif ids != nil {\n80: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n81: \t} else {\n82: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %v\", peerAddr)\n83: \t}\n84: \n85: \t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n86: \ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n87: \tcon := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n88: \n89: \t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected\n90: \t// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions\n91: \t// with push. According to the spec: \"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\n92: \t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\"\n93: \n94: \t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\n95: \t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\n96: \tgo s.receiveDelta(con, ids)\n97: \n98: \t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because\n99: \t// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout\n100: \t// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to\n101: \t// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the\n102: \t// initialization is complete.\n103: \t<-con.InitializedCh()\n104: \n105: \tfor {\n106: \t\t// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.\n107: \t\t// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)\n108: \t\t// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.\n109: \t\tselect {\n110: \t\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\n111: \t\t\tif ok {\n112: \t\t\t\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\n113: \t\t\t\t\treturn err\n114: \t\t\t\t}\n115: \t\t\t} else {\n116: \t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n117: \t\t\t\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\n118: \t\t\t}\n119: \t\tcase <-con.StopCh():\n120: \t\t\treturn nil\n121: \t\tdefault:\n122: \t\t}\n123: \t\t// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge\n124: \t\t// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;\n125: \t\t// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests\n126: \t\t// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.\n127: \t\tselect {\n128: \t\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\n129: \t\t\tif ok {\n130: \t\t\t\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\n131: \t\t\t\t\treturn err\n132: \t\t\t\t}\n133: \t\t\t} else {\n134: \t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n135: \t\t\t\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\n136: \t\t\t}\n137: \t\tcase ev := <-con.PushCh():\n138: \t\t\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n139: \t\t\terr := s.pushConnectionDelta(con, pushEv)\n140: \t\t\tpushEv.done()\n141: \t\t\tif err != nil {\n142: \t\t\t\treturn err\n143: \t\t\t}\n144: \t\tcase <-con.StopCh():\n145: \t\t\treturn nil\n146: \t\t}\n147: \t}\n148: }\n149: \n150: // Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\n151: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushConnectionDelta(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n152: \tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n153: \n154: \tif pushRequest.Full {\n155: \t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n156: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n157: \t}\n158: \n159: \tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n160: \tif !needsPush {\n161: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n162: \t\treturn nil\n163: \t}\n164: \n165: \t// Send pushes to all generators\n166: \t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n167: \twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n168: \tfor _, w := range wrl {\n169: \t\tif err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n170: \t\t\treturn err\n171: \t\t}\n172: \t}\n173: \n174: \tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n175: \treturn nil\n176: }\n177: \n178: func (s *DiscoveryServer) receiveDelta(con *Connection, identities []string) {\n179: \tdefer func() {\n180: \t\tclose(con.deltaReqChan)\n181: \t\tclose(con.ErrorCh())\n182: \t\t// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream\n183: \t\tselect {\n184: \t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n185: \t\tdefault:\n186: \t\t\tclose(con.InitializedCh())\n187: \t\t}\n188: \t}()\n189: \tfirstRequest := true\n190: \tfor {\n191: \t\treq, err := con.deltaStream.Recv()\n192: \t\tif err != nil {\n193: \t\t\tif istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {\n194: \t\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n195: \t\t\t\treturn\n196: \t\t\t}\n197: \t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\n198: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v\", con.Peer(), con.ID(), err)\n199: \t\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n200: \t\t\treturn\n201: \t\t}\n202: \t\t// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.\n203: \t\tif firstRequest {\n204: \t\t\t// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request\n205: \t\t\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n206: \t\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n207: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n208: \t\t\t}\n209: \t\t\tfirstRequest = false\n210: \t\t\tif req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == \"\" {\n211: \t\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, \"missing node information\").Err()\n212: \t\t\t\treturn\n213: \t\t\t}\n214: \t\t\tif err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {\n215: \t\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\n216: \t\t\t\treturn\n217: \t\t\t}\n218: \t\t\tdefer s.closeConnection(con)\n219: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: new delta connection for node:%s\", con.ID())\n220: \t\t}\n221: \n222: \t\tselect {\n223: \t\tcase con.deltaReqChan <- req:\n224: \t\tcase <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():\n225: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n226: \t\t\treturn\n227: \t\t}\n228: \t}\n229: }\n230: \n231: func (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {\n232: \tsendResonse := func() error {\n233: \t\tstart := time.Now()\n234: \t\tdefer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()\n235: \t\treturn conn.deltaStream.Send(res)\n236: \t}\n237: \terr := sendResonse()\n238: \tif err == nil {\n239: \t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n240: \t\t\tconn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n241: \t\t\t\tif wr == nil {\n242: \t\t\t\t\twr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}\n243: \t\t\t\t}\n244: \t\t\t\t// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though\n245: \t\t\t\tif newResourceNames != nil {\n246: \t\t\t\t\twr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames\n247: \t\t\t\t}\n248: \t\t\t\twr.NonceSent = res.Nonce\n249: \t\t\t\twr.LastSendTime = time.Now()\n250: \t\t\t\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\n251: \t\t\t\t\twr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)\n252: \t\t\t\t}\n253: \t\t\t\treturn wr\n254: \t\t\t})\n255: \t\t}\n256: \t} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\n257: \t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"Timeout writing %s: %v\", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))\n258: \t\txds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()\n259: \t}\n260: \treturn err\n261: }\n262: \n263: // processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n264: // handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n265: // protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\n266: func (s *DiscoveryServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n267: \tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n268: \tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s\", stype,\n269: \t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)\n270: \n271: \tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n272: \t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))\n273: \t\treturn nil\n274: \t}\n275: \tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n276: \t\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con,\n277: \t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},\n278: \t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n279: \t}\n280: \n281: \tshouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)\n282: \tif !shouldRespond {\n283: \t\treturn nil\n284: \t}\n285: \n286: \tsubs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)\n287: \trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n288: \t\tFull:   true,\n289: \t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n290: \t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n291: \n292: \t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n293: \t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n294: \t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n295: \t\tStart: con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n296: \t\tDelta: model.ResourceDelta{\n297: \t\t\t// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info\n298: \t\t\tSubscribed:   subs,\n299: \t\t\tUnsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete(\"*\"),\n300: \t\t},\n301: \t\tForced: true,\n302: \t}\n303: \t// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.\n304: \t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n305: \t// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).\n306: \tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n307: \t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n308: \t}\n309: \n310: \terr := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n311: \tif err != nil {\n312: \t\treturn err\n313: \t}\n314: \t// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.\n315: \t// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if\n316: \t// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).\n317: \t// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,\n318: \t// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed\n319: \t// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues\n320: \t// with EDS and SDS.\n321: \t// This can happen with the following sequence\n322: \t// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.\n323: \t// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.\n324: \t// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.\n325: \t// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends\n326: \t//    an EDS request for them.\n327: \t// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response\n328: \t// \t  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.\n329: \t// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.\n330: \tif req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {\n331: \t\treturn nil\n332: \t}\n333: \treturn s.forceEDSPush(con)\n334: }\n335: \n336: func (s *DiscoveryServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {\n337: \tif dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {\n338: \t\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n339: \t\t\tFull:   true,\n340: \t\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n341: \t\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),\n342: \t\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n343: \t\t\tForced: true,\n344: \t\t}\n345: \t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.\", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())\n346: \t\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)\n347: \t}\n348: \treturn nil\n349: }\n350: \n351: // shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol\n352: // using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.\n353: func shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {\n354: \tstype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)\n355: \n356: \t// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.\n357: \t// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info\n358: \t// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.\n359: \tif request.ErrorDetail != nil {\n360: \t\terrCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)\n361: \t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s\", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())\n362: \t\txds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())\n363: \t\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n364: \t\t\twr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()\n365: \t\t\treturn wr\n366: \t\t})\n367: \t\treturn false\n368: \t}\n369: \n370: \tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v\", stype, con.ID(),\n371: \t\trequest.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)\n372: \tpreviousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)\n373: \n374: \t// This can happen in two cases:\n375: \t// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod\n376: \t// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have\n377: \t// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either\n378: \t// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.\n379: \t// We should always respond with the current resource names.\n380: \tif previousInfo == nil {\n381: \t\tcon.proxy.Lock()\n382: \t\tdefer con.proxy.Unlock()\n383: \n384: \t\tif len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {\n385: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, len(request.InitialResourceVersions))\n386: \t\t} else {\n387: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: INIT %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n388: \t\t}\n389: \n390: \t\tres, wildcard, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, request)\n391: \t\tskip := request.TypeUrl == v3.AddressType && wildcard\n392: \t\tif skip {\n393: \t\t\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we do not store ResourceName.\n394: \t\t\t// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.\n395: \t\t\tres = nil\n396: \t\t}\n397: \t\tcon.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{\n398: \t\t\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\n399: \t\t\tResourceNames: res,\n400: \t\t\tWildcard:      wildcard,\n401: \t\t}\n402: \t\treturn true\n403: \t}\n404: \n405: \t// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.\n406: \t// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.\n407: \tif request.ResponseNonce != \"\" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {\n408: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s\", stype,\n409: \t\t\tcon.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)\n410: \t\txds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()\n411: \t\treturn false\n412: \t}\n413: \n414: \t// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.\n415: \t// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.\n416: \t// In this case response_nonce is empty.\n417: \tspontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == \"\"\n418: \n419: \tvar alwaysRespond bool\n420: \tvar subChanged bool\n421: \n422: \t// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.\n423: \tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n424: \t\twr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)\n425: \t\tif !spontaneousReq {\n426: \t\t\t// Clear last error, we got an ACK.\n427: \t\t\t// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info in place.\n428: \t\t\twr.LastError = \"\"\n429: \t\t\twr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce\n430: \t\t}\n431: \t\talwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond\n432: \t\twr.AlwaysRespond = false\n433: \t\treturn wr\n434: \t})\n435: \n436: \t// It is invalid in the below two cases:\n437: \t// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.\n438: \t// 2. subscribed resources changes from ACK.\n439: \tif spontaneousReq && !subChanged || !spontaneousReq && subChanged {\n440: \t\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)\n441: \t\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n442: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))\n443: \t\t}\n444: \t}\n445: \n446: \t// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce\n447: \t// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.\n448: \tif !subChanged {\n449: \t\t// We should always respond \"alwaysRespond\" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming\n450: \t\t// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.\n451: \t\tif alwaysRespond {\n452: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.\", stype, con.ID())\n453: \t\t\treturn true\n454: \t\t}\n455: \n456: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: ACK %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n457: \t\treturn false\n458: \t}\n459: \tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n460: \n461: \treturn true\n462: }\n463: \n464: // Push a Delta XDS resource for the given connection.\n465: func (s *DiscoveryServer) pushDeltaXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\n466: \tif w == nil {\n467: \t\treturn nil\n468: \t}\n469: \tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\n470: \tif gen == nil {\n471: \t\treturn nil\n472: \t}\n473: \tt0 := time.Now()\n474: \n475: \toriginalW := w\n476: \t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\n477: \t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\n478: \t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\n479: \t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\n480: \t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\n481: \tvar logFiltered string\n482: \tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {\n483: \t\t// Some types opt out of this and natively handle req.Delta\n484: \t\tlogFiltered = \" filtered:\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\n485: \t\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\n486: \t\t\tTypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,\n487: \t\t\tResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,\n488: \t\t}\n489: \t}\n490: \n491: \tvar res model.Resources\n492: \tvar deletedRes model.DeletedResources\n493: \tvar logdata model.XdsLogDetails\n494: \tvar usedDelta bool\n495: \tvar err error\n496: \tswitch g := gen.(type) {\n497: \tcase model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator:\n498: \t\tres, deletedRes, logdata, usedDelta, err = g.GenerateDeltas(con.proxy, req, w)\n499: \t\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\n500: \t\t\tfullRes, l, _ := g.Generate(con.proxy, originalW, req)\n501: \t\t\ts.compareDiff(con, originalW, fullRes, res, deletedRes, usedDelta, req.Delta, l.Incremental)\n502: \t\t}\n503: \tcase model.XdsResourceGenerator:\n504: \t\tres, logdata, err = g.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)\n505: \t}\n506: \tif err != nil || (res == nil && deletedRes == nil) {\n507: \t\treturn err\n508: \t}\n509: \tdefer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()\n510: \tresp := &discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse{\n511: \t\tControlPlane: ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),\n512: \t\tTypeUrl:      w.TypeUrl,\n513: \t\t// TODO: send different version for incremental eds\n514: \t\tSystemVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,\n515: \t\tNonce:             nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),\n516: \t\tResources:         res,\n517: \t}\n518: \tif usedDelta {\n519: \t\tresp.RemovedResources = deletedRes\n520: \t} else if req.Full {\n521: \t\t// similar to sotw\n522: \t\tremoved := w.ResourceNames.Copy()\n523: \t\tfor _, r := range res {\n524: \t\t\tremoved.Delete(r.Name)\n525: \t\t}\n526: \t\tresp.RemovedResources = sets.SortedList(removed)\n527: \t}\n528: \tvar newResourceNames sets.String\n529: \tif shouldSetWatchedResources(w) {\n530: \t\t// Set the new watched resources. Do not write to w directly, as it can be a copy from the 'filtered' logic above\n531: \t\tif usedDelta {\n532: \t\t\t// Apply the delta\n533: \t\t\tnewResourceNames = w.ResourceNames.Copy().\n534: \t\t\t\tDeleteAll(resp.RemovedResources...)\n535: \t\t\tfor _, r := range res {\n536: \t\t\t\tnewResourceNames.Insert(r.Name)\n537: \t\t\t}\n538: \t\t} else {\n539: \t\t\tnewResourceNames = resourceNamesSet(res)\n540: \t\t}\n541: \t}\n542: \tif neverRemoveDelta(w.TypeUrl) {\n543: \t\tresp.RemovedResources = nil\n544: \t}\n545: \tif len(resp.RemovedResources) > 0 {\n546: \t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%v REMOVE for node:%s %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.ID(), resp.RemovedResources)\n547: \t}\n548: \n549: \tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\n550: \tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\n551: \n552: \tptype := \"PUSH\"\n553: \tinfo := \"\"\n554: \tif logdata.Incremental {\n555: \t\tptype = \"PUSH INC\"\n556: \t}\n557: \tif len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {\n558: \t\tinfo = \" \" + logdata.AdditionalInfo\n559: \t}\n560: \tif len(logFiltered) > 0 {\n561: \t\tinfo += logFiltered\n562: \t}\n563: \n564: \tif err := con.sendDelta(resp, newResourceNames); err != nil {\n565: \t\tlogger := deltaLog.Debugf\n566: \t\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\n567: \t\t\tlogger = deltaLog.Warnf\n568: \t\t}\n569: \t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n570: \t\t\tlogger(\"%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v\",\n571: \t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\n572: \t\t}\n573: \t\treturn err\n574: \t}\n575: \n576: \tswitch {\n577: \tcase !req.Full:\n578: \t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n579: \t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\",\n580: \t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\n581: \t\t}\n582: \tdefault:\n583: \t\tdebug := \"\"\n584: \t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n585: \t\t\t// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.\n586: \t\t\tdebug = \" nonce:\" + resp.Nonce + \" version:\" + resp.SystemVersionInfo\n587: \t\t}\n588: \t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d removed:%d size:%v%s%s\",\n589: \t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), len(resp.RemovedResources),\n590: \t\t\tutil.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)\n591: \t}\n592: \n593: \treturn nil\n594: }\n595: \n596: func resourceNamesSet(res model.Resources) sets.Set[string] {\n597: \treturn sets.New(slices.Map(res, func(r *discovery.Resource) string {\n598: \t\treturn r.Name\n599: \t})...)\n600: }\n601: \n602: // requiresResourceNamesModification checks if a generator needs mutable access to w.ResourceNames.\n603: // This is used when resources are spontaneously pushed during Delta XDS\n604: func requiresResourceNamesModification(url string) bool {\n605: \treturn url == v3.AddressType || url == v3.WorkloadType\n606: }\n607: \n608: // neverRemoveDelta checks During\n\t\t// loading request, concurrency=1. Once that request is done, concurrency is enabled.\n\t\t// However, the XDS stream is long lived, so the first request would block all others. As a\n\t\t// result, we should exit the first request immediately; clients will retry.\n\t\tfirstRequest.Store(false)\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unavailable, \"server warmup not complete; try again\")\n\t}\n\t// Check if server is ready to accept clients and process new requests.\n\t// Currently ready means caches have been synced and hence can build\n\t// clusters correctly. Without this check, InitContext() call below would\n\t// initialize with empty config, leading to reconnected Envoys losing\n\t// configuration. This is an additional safety check inaddition to adding\n\t// cachesSynced logic to readiness probe to handle cases where kube-proxy\n\t// ip tables update latencies.\n\t// See https://github.com/istio/istio/issues/25495.\n\tif !s.IsServerReady() {\n\t\treturn errors.New(\"server is not ready to serve discovery information\")\n\t}\n\n\tctx := stream.Context()\n\tpeerAddr := \"0.0.0.0\"\n\tif peerInfo, ok := peer.FromContext(ctx); ok {\n\t\tpeerAddr = peerInfo.Addr.String()\n\t}\n\n\tif err := s.WaitForRequestLimit(stream.Context()); err != nil {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q exceeded rate limit: %v\", peerAddr, err)\n\t\treturn status.Errorf(codes.ResourceExhausted, \"request rate limit exceeded: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tids, err := s.authenticate(ctx)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())\n\t}\n\tif ids != nil {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Authenticated XDS: %v with identity %v\", peerAddr, ids)\n\t} else {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Unauthenticated XDS: %v\", peerAddr)\n\t}\n\n\t// InitContext returns immediately if the context was already initialized.\n\ts.globalPushContext().InitContext(s.Env, nil, nil)\n\tcon := newDeltaConnection(peerAddr, stream)\n\n\t// Do not call: defer close(con.pushChannel). The push channel will be garbage collected\n\t// when the connection is no longer used. Closing the channel can cause subtle race conditions\n\t// with push. According to the spec: \"It's only necessary to close a channel when it is important\n\t// to tell the receiving goroutines that all data have been sent.\"\n\n\t// Block until either a request is received or a push is triggered.\n\t// We need 2 go routines because 'read' blocks in Recv().\n\tgo s.receiveDelta(con, ids)\n\n\t// Wait for the proxy to be fully initialized before we start serving traffic. Because\n\t// initialization doesn't have dependencies that will block, there is no need to add any timeout\n\t// here. Prior to this explicit wait, we were implicitly waiting by receive() not sending to\n\t// reqChannel and the connection not being enqueued for pushes to pushChannel until the\n\t// initialization is complete.\n\t<-con.InitializedCh()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\t// Go select{} statements are not ordered; the same channel can be chosen many times.\n\t\t// For requests, these are higher priority (client may be blocked on startup until these are done)\n\t\t// and often very cheap to handle (simple ACK), so we check it first.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n\t\t\t\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-con.StopCh():\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\t\t// If there wasn't already a request, poll for requests and pushes. Note: if we have a huge\n\t\t// amount of incoming requests, we may still send some pushes, as we do not `continue` above;\n\t\t// however, requests will be handled ~2x as much as pushes. This ensures a wave of requests\n\t\t// cannot completely starve pushes. However, this scenario is unlikely.\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase req, ok := <-con.deltaReqChan:\n\t\t\tif ok {\n\t\t\t\tif err := s.processDeltaRequest(req, con); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Remote side closed connection or error processing the request.\n\t\t\t\treturn <-con.ErrorCh()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase ev := <-con.PushCh():\n\t\t\tpushEv := ev.(*Event)\n\t\t\terr := s.pushConnectionDelta(con, pushEv)\n\t\t\tpushEv.done()\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t\t}\n\t\tcase <-con.StopCh():\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// Compute and send the new configuration for a connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushConnectionDelta(con *Connection, pushEv *Event) error {\n\tpushRequest := pushEv.pushRequest\n\n\tif pushRequest.Full {\n\t\t// Update Proxy with current information.\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\t}\n\n\tpushRequest, needsPush := s.ProxyNeedsPush(con.proxy, pushRequest)\n\tif !needsPush {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"Skipping push to %v, no updates required\", con.ID())\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Send pushes to all generators\n\t// Each Generator is responsible for determining if the push event requires a push\n\twrl := con.watchedResourcesByOrder()\n\tfor _, w := range wrl {\n\t\tif err := s.pushDeltaXds(con, w, pushRequest); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn err\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tproxiesConvergeDelay.Record(time.Since(pushRequest.Start).Seconds())\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) receiveDelta(con *Connection, identities []string) {\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tclose(con.deltaReqChan)\n\t\tclose(con.ErrorCh())\n\t\t// Close the initialized channel, if its not already closed, to prevent blocking the stream\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-con.InitializedCh():\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tclose(con.InitializedCh())\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\tfirstRequest := true\n\tfor {\n\t\treq, err := con.deltaStream.Recv()\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tif istiogrpc.GRPCErrorType(err) != istiogrpc.UnexpectedError {\n\t\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with error: %v\", con.Peer(), con.ID(), err)\n\t\t\txds.TotalXDSInternalErrors.Increment()\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t\t// This should be only set for the first request. The node id may not be set - for example malicious clients.\n\t\tif firstRequest {\n\t\t\t// probe happens before envoy sends first xDS request\n\t\t\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"ADS: %q %s send health check probe before normal xDS request\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tfirstRequest = false\n\t\t\tif req.Node == nil || req.Node.Id == \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- status.New(codes.InvalidArgument, \"missing node information\").Err()\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := s.initConnection(req.Node, con, identities); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tcon.ErrorCh() <- err\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer s.closeConnection(con)\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: new delta connection for node:%s\", con.ID())\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase con.deltaReqChan <- req:\n\t\tcase <-con.deltaStream.Context().Done():\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS: %q %s terminated with stream closed\", con.Peer(), con.ID())\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (conn *Connection) sendDelta(res *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse, newResourceNames sets.String) error {\n\tsendResonse := func() error {\n\t\tstart := time.Now()\n\t\tdefer func() { xds.RecordSendTime(time.Since(start)) }()\n\t\treturn conn.deltaStream.Send(res)\n\t}\n\terr := sendResonse()\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(res.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\t\tconn.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(res.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n\t\t\t\tif wr == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\twr = &model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: res.TypeUrl}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t// some resources dynamically update ResourceNames. Most don't though\n\t\t\t\tif newResourceNames != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\twr.ResourceNames = newResourceNames\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\twr.NonceSent = res.Nonce\n\t\t\t\twr.LastSendTime = time.Now()\n\t\t\t\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\n\t\t\t\t\twr.LastResources = applyDelta(wr.LastResources, res)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\treturn wr\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t} else if status.Convert(err).Code() == codes.DeadlineExceeded {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"Timeout writing %s: %v\", conn.ID(), v3.GetShortType(res.TypeUrl))\n\t\txds.ResponseWriteTimeouts.Increment()\n\t}\n\treturn err\n}\n\n// processDeltaRequest is handling one request. This is currently called from the 'main' thread, which also\n// handles 'push' requests and close - the code will eventually call the 'push' code, and it needs more mutex\n// protection. Original code avoided the mutexes by doing both 'push' and 'process requests' in same thread.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) processDeltaRequest(req *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, con *Connection) error {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(req.TypeUrl)\n\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s resources sub:%d unsub:%d nonce:%s\", stype,\n\t\tcon.ID(), len(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe), len(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe), req.ResponseNonce)\n\n\tif req.TypeUrl == v3.HealthInfoType {\n\t\ts.handleWorkloadHealthcheck(con.proxy, deltaToSotwRequest(req))\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tif strings.HasPrefix(req.TypeUrl, v3.DebugType) {\n\t\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con,\n\t\t\t&model.WatchedResource{TypeUrl: req.TypeUrl, ResourceNames: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesSubscribe...)},\n\t\t\t&model.PushRequest{Full: true, Push: con.proxy.LastPushContext, Forced: true})\n\t}\n\n\tshouldRespond := shouldRespondDelta(con, req)\n\tif !shouldRespond {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tsubs, _, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, req)\n\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.ProxyRequest),\n\n\t\t// The usage of LastPushTime (rather than time.Now()), is critical here for correctness; This time\n\t\t// is used by the XDS cache to determine if a entry is stale. If we use Now() with an old push context,\n\t\t// we may end up overriding active cache entries with stale ones.\n\t\tStart: con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\tDelta: model.ResourceDelta{\n\t\t\t// Record sub/unsub, but drop synthetic wildcard info\n\t\t\tSubscribed:   subs,\n\t\t\tUnsubscribed: sets.New(req.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe...).Delete(\"*\"),\n\t\t},\n\t\tForced: true,\n\t}\n\t// SidecarScope for the proxy may has not been updated based on this pushContext.\n\t// It can happen when `processRequest` comes after push context has been updated(s.initPushContext),\n\t// but before proxy's SidecarScope has been updated(s.updateProxy).\n\tif con.proxy.SidecarScope != nil && con.proxy.SidecarScope.Version != request.Push.PushVersion {\n\t\ts.computeProxyState(con.proxy, request)\n\t}\n\n\terr := s.pushDeltaXds(con, con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(req.TypeUrl), request)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\t// Anytime we get a CDS request on reconnect, we should always push EDS as well.\n\t// It is always the server's responsibility to send EDS after CDS, regardless if\n\t// Envoy asks for it or not (See https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/33607 for more details).\n\t// Without this logic, there are cases where the clusters we send could stay warming forever,\n\t// expecting an EDS response. Note that in SotW, Envoy sends an EDS request after the delayed\n\t// CDS request; however, this is not guaranteed in delta, and has been observed to cause issues\n\t// with EDS and SDS.\n\t// This can happen with the following sequence\n\t// 1. Envoy disconnects and reconnects to Istiod.\n\t// 2. Envoy sends EDS request and we respond with it.\n\t// 3. Envoy sends CDS request and we respond with clusters.\n\t// 4. Envoy detects a change in cluster state and tries to warm those clusters but never sends\n\t//    an EDS request for them.\n\t// 5. Therefore, any initial CDS request should always trigger an EDS response\n\t// \t  to let Envoy finish cluster warming.\n\t// Refer to https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/13009 for some more details on this type of issues.\n\tif req.TypeUrl != v3.ClusterType {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn s.forceEDSPush(con)\n}\n\nfunc (s *XDSServer) forceEDSPush(con *Connection) error {\n\tif dwr := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(v3.EndpointType); dwr != nil {\n\t\trequest := &model.PushRequest{\n\t\t\tFull:   true,\n\t\t\tPush:   con.proxy.LastPushContext,\n\t\t\tReason: model.NewReasonStats(model.DependentResource),\n\t\t\tStart:  con.proxy.LastPushTime,\n\t\t\tForced: true,\n\t\t}\n\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS:%s: FORCE %s PUSH for warming.\", v3.GetShortType(v3.EndpointType), con.ID())\n\t\treturn s.pushDeltaXds(con, dwr, request)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// shouldRespondDelta determines whether this request needs to be responded back. It applies the ack/nack rules as per xds protocol\n// using WatchedResource for previous state and discovery request for the current state.\nfunc shouldRespondDelta(con *Connection, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) bool {\n\tstype := v3.GetShortType(request.TypeUrl)\n\n\t// If there is an error in request that means previous response is erroneous.\n\t// We do not have to respond in that case. In this case request's version info\n\t// will be different from the version sent. But it is fragile to rely on that.\n\tif request.ErrorDetail != nil {\n\t\terrCode := codes.Code(request.ErrorDetail.Code)\n\t\tdeltaLog.Warnf(\"ADS:%s: ACK ERROR %s %s:%s\", stype, con.ID(), errCode.String(), request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage())\n\t\txds.IncrementXDSRejects(request.TypeUrl, con.proxy.ID, errCode.String())\n\t\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n\t\t\twr.LastError = request.ErrorDetail.GetMessage()\n\t\t\treturn wr\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s REQUEST %v: sub:%v unsub:%v initial:%v\", stype, con.ID(),\n\t\trequest.ResourceNamesSubscribe, request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe, request.InitialResourceVersions)\n\tpreviousInfo := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl)\n\n\t// This can happen in two cases:\n\t// 1. Envoy initially send request to Istiod\n\t// 2. Envoy reconnect to Istiod i.e. Istiod does not have\n\t// information about this typeUrl, but Envoy sends response nonce - either\n\t// because Istiod is restarted or Envoy disconnects and reconnects.\n\t// We should always respond with the current resource names.\n\tif previousInfo == nil {\n\t\tcon.proxy.Lock()\n\t\tdefer con.proxy.Unlock()\n\n\t\tif len(request.InitialResourceVersions) > 0 {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: RECONNECT %s %s resources:%v\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, len(request.InitialResourceVersions))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: INIT %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tres, wildcard, _ := deltaWatchedResources(nil, request)\n\t\tskip := request.TypeUrl == v3.AddressType && wildcard\n\t\tif skip {\n\t\t\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we do not store ResourceName.\n\t\t\t// See the workload generator for more information on why we don't use this.\n\t\t\tres = nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tcon.proxy.WatchedResources[request.TypeUrl] = &model.WatchedResource{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\n\t\t\tResourceNames: res,\n\t\t\tWildcard:      wildcard,\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is mismatch in the nonce, that is a case of expired/stale nonce.\n\t// A nonce becomes stale following a newer nonce being sent to Envoy.\n\tif request.ResponseNonce != \"\" && request.ResponseNonce != previousInfo.NonceSent {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: REQ %s Expired nonce received %s, sent %s\", stype,\n\t\t\tcon.ID(), request.ResponseNonce, previousInfo.NonceSent)\n\t\txds.ExpiredNonce.With(typeTag.Value(v3.GetMetricType(request.TypeUrl))).Increment()\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Spontaneous DeltaDiscoveryRequests from the client.\n\t// This can be done to dynamically add or remove elements from the tracked resource_names set.\n\t// In this case response_nonce is empty.\n\tspontaneousReq := request.ResponseNonce == \"\"\n\n\tvar alwaysRespond bool\n\tvar subChanged bool\n\n\t// Update resource names, and record ACK if required.\n\tcon.proxy.UpdateWatchedResource(request.TypeUrl, func(wr *model.WatchedResource) *model.WatchedResource {\n\t\twr.ResourceNames, _, subChanged = deltaWatchedResources(wr.ResourceNames, request)\n\t\tif !spontaneousReq {\n\t\t\t// Clear last error, we got an ACK.\n\t\t\t// Otherwise, this is just a change in resource subscription, so leave the last ACK info in place.\n\t\t\twr.LastError = \"\"\n\t\t\twr.NonceAcked = request.ResponseNonce\n\t\t}\n\t\talwaysRespond = wr.AlwaysRespond\n\t\twr.AlwaysRespond = false\n\t\treturn wr\n\t})\n\n\t// It is invalid in the below two cases:\n\t// 1. no subscribed resources change from spontaneous delta request.\n\t// 2. subscribed resources changes from ACK.\n\tif spontaneousReq && !subChanged || !spontaneousReq && subChanged {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Errorf(\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged)\n\t\tif features.EnableUnsafeAssertions {\n\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"ADS:%s: Subscribed resources check mismatch: %v vs %v\", stype, spontaneousReq, subChanged))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Envoy can send two DiscoveryRequests with same version and nonce\n\t// when it detects a new resource. We should respond if they change.\n\tif !subChanged {\n\t\t// We should always respond \"alwaysRespond\" marked requests to let Envoy finish warming\n\t\t// even though Nonce match and it looks like an ACK.\n\t\tif alwaysRespond {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"ADS:%s: FORCE RESPONSE %s for warming.\", stype, con.ID())\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: ACK %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: RESOURCE CHANGE %s %s\", stype, con.ID(), request.ResponseNonce)\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// Push a Delta XDS resource for the given connection.\nfunc (s *XDSServer) pushDeltaXds(con *Connection, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) error {\n\tif w == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tgen := s.findGenerator(w.TypeUrl, con)\n\tif gen == nil {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\tt0 := time.Now()\n\n\toriginalW := w\n\t// If delta is set, client is requesting new resources or removing old ones. We should just generate the\n\t// new resources it needs, rather than the entire set of known resources.\n\t// Note: we do not need to account for unsubscribed resources as these are handled by parent removal;\n\t// See https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#deleting-resources.\n\t// This means if there are only removals, we will not respond.\n\tvar logFiltered string\n\tif !req.Delta.IsEmpty() && !requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {\n\t\t// Some types opt out of this and natively handle req.Delta\n\t\tlogFiltered = \" filtered:\" + strconv.Itoa(len(w.ResourceNames)-len(req.Delta.Subscribed))\n\t\tw = &model.WatchedResource{\n\t\t\tTypeUrl:       w.TypeUrl,\n\t\t\tResourceNames: req.Delta.Subscribed,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tvar res model.Resources\n\tvar deletedRes model.DeletedResources\n\tvar logdata model.XdsLogDetails\n\tvar usedDelta bool\n\tvar err error\n\tswitch g := gen.(type) {\n\tcase model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator:\n\t\tres, deletedRes, logdata, usedDelta, err = g.GenerateDeltas(con.proxy, req, w)\n\t\tif features.EnableUnsafeDeltaTest {\n\t\t\tfullRes, l, _ := g.Generate(con.proxy, originalW, req)\n\t\t\ts.compareDiff(con, originalW, fullRes, res, deletedRes, usedDelta, req.Delta, l.Incremental)\n\t\t}\n\tcase model.XdsResourceGenerator:\n\t\tres, logdata, err = g.Generate(con.proxy, w, req)\n\t}\n\tif err != nil || (res == nil && deletedRes == nil) {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\tdefer func() { recordPushTime(w.TypeUrl, time.Since(t0)) }()\n\tresp := &discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse{\n\t\tControlPlane:       ControlPlane(w.TypeUrl),\n\t\tTypeUrl:            w.TypeUrl,\n\t\tSystemVersionInfo: req.Push.PushVersion,\n\t\tNonce:              nonce(req.Push.PushVersion),\n\t\tResources:          res,\n\t}\n\tif usedDelta {\n\t\tresp.RemovedResources = deletedRes\n\t} else if req.Full {\n\t\t// similar to sotw\n\t\tremoved := w.ResourceNames.Copy()\n\t\tfor _, r := range res {\n\t\t\tremoved.Delete(r.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t\tresp.RemovedResources = sets.SortedList(removed)\n\t}\n\tvar newResourceNames sets.String\n\tif shouldSetWatchedResources(w) {\n\t\t// Set the new watched resources. Do not write to w directly, as it can be a copy from the 'filtered' logic above\n\t\tif usedDelta {\n\t\t\t// Apply the delta\n\t\t\tnewResourceNames = w.ResourceNames.Copy().\n\t\t\t\tDeleteAll(resp.RemovedResources...)\n\t\t\tfor _, r := range res {\n\t\t\t\tnewResourceNames.Insert(r.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tnewResourceNames = resourceNamesSet(res)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif neverRemoveDelta(w.TypeUrl) {\n\t\tresp.RemovedResources = nil\n\t}\n\tif len(resp.RemovedResources) > 0 {\n\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"ADS:%v REMOVE for node:%s %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.ID(), resp.RemovedResources)\n\t}\n\n\tconfigSize := ResourceSize(res)\n\tconfigSizeBytes.With(typeTag.Value(w.TypeUrl)).Record(float64(configSize))\n\n\tptype := \"PUSH\"\n\tinfo := \"\"\n\tif logdata.Incremental {\n\t\tptype = \"PUSH INC\"\n\t}\n\tif len(logdata.AdditionalInfo) > 0 {\n\t\tinfo = \" \" + logdata.AdditionalInfo\n\t}\n\tif len(logFiltered) > 0 {\n\t\tinfo += logFiltered\n\t}\n\n\tif err := con.sendDelta(resp, newResourceNames); err != nil {\n\t\tlogger := deltaLog.Debugf\n\t\tif recordSendError(w.TypeUrl, err) {\n\t\t\tlogger = deltaLog.Warnf\n\t\t}\n\t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\tlogger(\"%s: Send failure for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s: %v\",\n\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info, err)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase !req.Full:\n\t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\tdeltaLog.Debugf(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d size:%s%s\",\n\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), util.ByteCount(configSize), info)\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\tdebug := \"\"\n\t\tif deltaLog.DebugEnabled() {\n\t\t\t// Add additional information to logs when debug mode enabled.\n\t\t\tdebug = \" nonce:\" + resp.Nonce + \" version:\" + resp.SystemVersionInfo\n\t\t}\n\t\tdeltaLog.Infof(\"%s: %s%s for node:%s resources:%d removed:%d size:%v%s%s\",\n\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), ptype, req.PushReason(), con.proxy.ID, len(res), len(resp.RemovedResources),\n\t\t\tutil.ByteCount(ResourceSize(res)), info, debug)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\nfunc resourceNamesSet(res model.Resources) sets.Set[string] {\n\treturn sets.New(slices.Map(res, func(r *discovery.Resource) string {\n\t\treturn r.Name\n\t})...)\n}\n\n// requiresResourceNamesModification checks if a generator needs mutable access to w.ResourceNames.\n// This is used when resources are spontaneously pushed during Delta XDS\nfunc requiresResourceNamesModification(url string) bool {\n\treturn url == v3.AddressType || url == v3.WorkloadType\n}\n\n// neverRemoveDelta checks if a type should never remove resources\nfunc neverRemoveDelta(url string) bool {\n\t// https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/32823\n\t// We want to garbage collect extensions when they are no longer referenced, rather than delete immediately\n\treturn url == v3.ExtensionConfigurationType\n}\n\n// shouldSetWatchedResources indicates whether we should set the watched resources for a given type.\n// for some type like `Address` we customly handle it in the generator\nfunc shouldSetWatchedResources(w *model.WatchedResource) bool {\n\tif requiresResourceNamesModification(w.TypeUrl) {\n\t\t// These handle it directly in the generator\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\t// Else fallback based on type\n\treturn xds.IsWildcardTypeURL(w.TypeUrl)\n}\n\nfunc newDeltaConnection(peerAddr string, stream DeltaDiscoveryStream) *Connection {\n\treturn &Connection{\n\t\tConnection:   xds.NewConnection(peerAddr, nil),\n\t\tdeltaStream:  stream,\n\t\tdeltaReqChan: make(chan *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest, 1),\n\t}\n}\n\n// To satisfy methods that need DiscoveryRequest. Not suitable for real usage\nfunc deltaToSotwRequest(request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) *discovery.DiscoveryRequest {\n\treturn &discovery.DiscoveryRequest{\n\t\tNode:          request.Node,\n\t\tResourceNames: request.ResourceNamesSubscribe,\n\t\tTypeUrl:       request.TypeUrl,\n\t\tResponseNonce: request.ResponseNonce,\n\t\tErrorDetail:   request.ErrorDetail,\n\t}\n}\n\n// deltaWatchedResources returns current watched resources of delta xds\nfunc deltaWatchedResources(existing sets.String, request *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryRequest) (sets.String, bool, bool) {\n\tres := existing\n\tif res == nil {\n\t\tres = sets.New[string]()\n\t}\n\tchanged := false\n\tfor _, r := range request.ResourceNamesSubscribe {\n\t\tif !res.InsertContains(r) {\n\t\t\tchanged = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\t// This is set by Envoy on first request on reconnection so that we are aware of what Envoy knows\n\t// and can continue the xDS session properly.\n\tfor r := range request.InitialResourceVersions {\n\t\tif !res.InsertContains(r) {\n\t\t\tchanged = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor _, r := range request.ResourceNamesUnsubscribe {\n\t\tif res.DeleteContains(r) {\n\t\t\tchanged = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\twildcard := false\n\t// A request is wildcard if they explicitly subscribe to \"*\" or subscribe to nothing\n\tif res.Contains(\"*\") {\n\t\twildcard = true\n\t\tres.Delete(\"*\")\n\t}\n\t// \"if the client sends a request but has never explicitly subscribed to any resource names, the\n\t// server should treat that identically to how it would treat the client having explicitly\n\t// subscribed to *\"\n\t// NOTE: this means you cannot subscribe to nothing, which is useful for on-demand loading; to workaround this\n\t// Istio clients will send and initial request both subscribing+unsubscribing to `*`.\n\tif len(request.ResourceNamesSubscribe) == 0 {\n\t\twildcard = true\n\t}\n\treturn res, wildcard, changed\n}\nDELTA_EOF\n","description":"Create delta.go with XDSServer naming"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":16349,"cache_read_input_tokens":93087,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":16349},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"731dac43-d059-4b49-b6ef-e931483c341b","uuid":"2e5362bd-70d8-424f-9ab6-ae8fbd9e410b"}

This avoids high cost\n\t// proto.Size, at the expense of slightly under counting.\n\tsize := 0\n\tfor _, r := range r {\n\t\tsize += len(r.Resource.Value)\n\t}\n\treturn size\n}\n\n// xdsNeedsPush checks for the common cases whether we need to push or not.\nfunc xdsNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest, proxy *model.Proxy) (needsPush, definitive bool) {\n\tif proxy.Type == model.Ztunnel {\n\t\t// Not supported for ztunnel\n\t\treturn false, true\n\t}\n\tif req == nil {\n\t\treturn true, true\n\t}\n\t// If none set, we will always push\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\treturn true, true\n\t}\n\n\t// We can not definitively say if we need to push or not based on generic checks, so we will push based\n\t// on the specific typeURL checks.\n\treturn false, false\n}\n\n// waypointNeedsPush checks if an incremental push is needed for CDS and LDS which normally do a full push.\nfunc waypointNeedsPush(req *model.PushRequest) bool {\n\t// Waypoint proxies needs to be pushed for LDS and CDS on kind.Address changes.\n\t// Waypoint proxies have a matcher against pod IPs in them. Historically, any LDS change would do a full\n\t// push, recomputing push context. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\\n39: //\\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses.
