This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\\n39: //\\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\\n44: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n45: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n46: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n48: \\taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\\n49: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n50: \\tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\\n51: \\t\\t// Nothing changed...\\n52: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n53: \\t}\\n54: \\n55: \\tif !w.Wildcard {\\n56: \\t\\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\\n57: \\t}\\n58: \\n59: \\treqAddresses := addresses\\n60: \\tif isReq {\\n61: \\t\\treqAddresses = nil\\n62: \\t}\\n63: \\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)\\n64: \\t// Note: while \\\"removed\\\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\\n65: \\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\\n66: \\thave := sets.New[string]()\\n67: \\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\\n68: \\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\\n69: \\t\\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)\\n70: \\t}\\n71: \\n72: \\tif isReq {\\n73: \\t\\t// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.\\n74: \\t\\t// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.\\n75: \\t\\t// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:\\n76: \\t\\t// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).\\n77: \\t\\tremoved = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)\\n78: \\t}\\n79: \\n80: \\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.\\n81: \\t// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).\\n82: \\t// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from\\n83: \\t// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.\\n84: \\n85: \\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\\n86: }\\n87: \\n88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {\\n89: \\tn := addr.ResourceName()\\n90: \\thave.Insert(n)\\n91: \\tswitch requestedType {\\n92: \\tcase v3.WorkloadType:\\n93: \\t\\tif addr.GetWorkload() != nil {\\n94: \\t\\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n95: \\t\\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n96: \\t\\t\\t\\tAliases:  aliases,\\n97: \\t\\t\\t\\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal\\n98: \\t\\t\\t})\\n99: \\t\\t}\\n100: \\tcase v3.AddressType:\\n101: \\t\\tproto := addr.Marshaled\\n102: \\t\\tif proto == nil {\\n103: \\t\\t\\tproto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)\\n104: \\t\\t}\\n105: \\n106: \\t\\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\\n107: \\t\\t\\tName:     n,\\n108: \\t\\t\\tAliases:  aliases,\\n109: \\t\\t\\tResource: proto,\\n110: \\t\\t})\\n111: \\t}\\n112: \\treturn resources\\n113: }\\n114: \\n115: func (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\\n116: \\tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\\n117: \\treturn resources, details, err\\n118: }\\n119: \\n120: func (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(\\n121: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n122: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n123: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n124: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\\n125: \\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\\n126: \\tsubs := w.ResourceNames\\n127: \\n128: \\tvar addresses sets.String\\n129: \\tif isReq {\\n130: \\t\\t// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address\\n131: \\t\\t// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.\\n132: \\t\\t// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.\\n133: \\t\\taddresses = req.Delta.Subscribed\\n134: \\t} else {\\n135: \\t\\t// this is from the external triggers instead of request\\n136: \\t\\t// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated\\n137: \\t\\taddresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)\\n138: \\t}\\n139: \\n140: \\t// We only need this for on-demand. This allows us to subscribe the client to resources they\\n141: \\t// didn't explicitly request.\\n142: \\t// For wildcard, they subscribe to everything already.\\n143: \\tadditional := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AdditionalPodSubscriptions(proxy, addresses, subs)\\n144: \\tif addresses == nil {\\n145: \\t\\taddresses = additional\\n146: \\t} else {\\n147: \\t\\taddresses.Merge(additional)\\n148: \\t}\\n149: \\n150: \\tif len(addresses) == 0 {\\n151: \\t\\tif isReq {\\n152: \\t\\t\\t// We need to respond for requests, even if we have nothing to respond with\\n153: \\t\\t\\treturn make(model.Resources, 0), nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n154: \\t\\t}\\n155: \\t\\t// For NOP pushes, no need\\n156: \\t\\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\\n157: \\t}\\n158: \\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(addresses)\\n159: \\t// Note: while \\\"removed\\\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\\n160: \\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\\n161: \\thave := sets.New[string]()\\n162: \\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\\n163: \\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\\n164: \\t\\taliases := addr.Aliases()\\n165: \\t\\tremoved.DeleteAll(aliases...)\\n166: \\t\\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, aliases, have, resources)\\n167: \\t}\\n168: \\n169: \\tproxy.Lock()\\n170: \\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\\n171: \\t// For on-demand, we may have requested a VIP but gotten Pod IPs back. We need to update\\n172: \\t// the internal book-keeping to subscribe to the Pods, so that we push updates to those Pods.\\n173: \\tw.ResourceNames = subs.Merge(have)\\n174: \\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\\n175: }\\n176: \\n177: type WorkloadRBACGenerator struct {\\n178: \\tServer *DiscoveryServer\\n179: }\\n180: \\n181: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\\n182: \\tproxy *model.Proxy,\\n183: \\treq *model.PushRequest,\\n184: \\tw *model.WatchedResource,\\n185: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, everything\\n190: \\t\\texpected.Merge(w.ResourceNames)\\n191: \\t} else {\\n192: \\t\\t// The ambient store will send all of these as kind.AuthorizationPolicy, even if generated from PeerAuthentication,\\n193: \\t\\t// so we can only fetch these ones.\\n194: \\t\\tupdatedPolicies = model.ConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.AuthorizationPolicy)\\n195: \\n196: \\t\\tif \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/istio--f8af3cae@3a22a44f97d470ed898e4dfb3b17913cb295c7bf/-/blob/pilot/pkg/xds/workload.go\"}","structuredContent":{"content":"1: // and\n13: // limitations under the License.\n14: \n15: package xds\n16: \n17: import (\n18: \tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n19: \n20: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n21: \t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/util/protoconv\"\n22: \tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n23: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/config/schema/kind\"\n24: \t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n25: )\n26: \n27: type WorkloadGenerator struct {\n28: \tServer *DiscoveryServer\n29: }\n30: \n31: var (\n32: \t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadGenerator{}\n33: \t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadGenerator{}\n34: )\n35: \n36: // GenerateDeltas computes Workload resources. This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\n37: // and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\n38: // resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\n39: //\n40: // Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\n41: // This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\n42: // On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\n43: func (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n44: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n45: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n46: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n47: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n48: \taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\n49: \tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n50: \tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\n51: \t\t// Nothing changed...\n52: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n53: \t}\n54: \n55: \tif !w.Wildcard {\n56: \t\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\n57: \t}\n58: \n59: \treqAddresses := addresses\n60: \tif isReq {\n61: \t\treqAddresses = nil\n62: \t}\n63: \taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)\n64: \t// Note: while \"removed\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\n65: \t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\n66: \thave := sets.New[string]()\n67: \tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\n68: \tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n69: \t\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)\n70: \t}\n71: \n72: \tif isReq {\n73: \t\t// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.\n74: \t\t// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.\n75: \t\t// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:\n76: \t\t// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).\n77: \t\tremoved = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)\n78: \t}\n79: \n80: \t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.\n81: \t// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).\n82: \t// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from\n83: \t// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.\n84: \n85: \treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n86: }\n87: \n88: func appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {\n89: \tn := addr.ResourceName()\n90: \thave.Insert(n)\n91: \tswitch requestedType {\n92: \tcase v3.WorkloadType:\n93: \t\tif addr.GetWorkload() != nil {\n94: \t\t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n95: \t\t\t\tName:     n,\n96: \t\t\t\tAliases:  aliases,\n97: \t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal\n98: \t\t\t})\n99: \t\t}\n100: \tcase v3.AddressType:\n101: \t\tproto := addr.Marshaled\n102: \t\tif proto == nil {\n103: \t\t\tproto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)\n104: \t\t}\n105: \n106: \t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n107: \t\t\tName:     n,\n108: \t\t\tAliases:  aliases,\n109: \t\t\tResource: proto,\n110: \t\t})\n111: \t}\n112: \treturn resources\n113: }\n114: \n115: func (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n116: \tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\n117: \treturn resources, details, err\n118: }\n119: \n120: func (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(\n121: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n122: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n123: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n124: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n125: \tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n126: \tsubs := w.ResourceNames\n127: \n128: \tvar addresses sets.String\n129: \tif isReq {\n130: \t\t// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address\n131: \t\t// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.\n132: \t\t// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.\n133: \t\taddresses = req.Delta.Subscribed\n134: \t} else {\n135: \t\t// this is from the external triggers instead of request\n136: \t\t// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated\n137: \t\taddresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)\n138: \t}\n139: \n140: \t// We only need this for on-demand. This allows us to subscribe the client to resources they\n141: \t// didn't explicitly request.\n142: \t// For wildcard, they subscribe to everything already.\n143: \tadditional := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AdditionalPodSubscriptions(proxy, addresses, subs)\n144: \tif addresses == nil {\n145: \t\taddresses = additional\n146: \t} else {\n147: \t\taddresses.Merge(additional)\n148: \t}\n149: \n150: \tif len(addresses) == 0 {\n151: \t\tif isReq {\n152: \t\t\t// We need to respond for requests, even if we have nothing to respond with\n153: \t\t\treturn make(model.Resources, 0), nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n154: \t\t}\n155: \t\t// For NOP pushes, no need\n156: \t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n157: \t}\n158: \taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(addresses)\n159: \t// Note: while \"removed\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\n160: \t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\n161: \thave := sets.New[string]()\n162: \tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\n163: \tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n164: \t\taliases := addr.Aliases()\n165: \t\tremoved.DeleteAll(aliases...)\n166: \t\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, aliases, have, resources)\n167: \t}\n168: \n169: \tproxy.Lock()\n170: \tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n171: \t// For on-demand, we may have requested a VIP but gotten Pod IPs back. We need to update\n172: \t// the internal book-keeping to subscribe to the Pods, so that we push updates to those Pods.\n173: \tw.ResourceNames = subs.Merge(have)\n174: \treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n175: }\n176: \n177: type WorkloadRBACGenerator struct {\n178: \tServer *DiscoveryServer\n179: }\n180: \n181: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n182: \tproxy *model.Proxy,\n183: \treq *model.PushRequest,\n184: \tw *model.WatchedResource,\n185: ) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n186: \tvar updatedPolicies sets.Set[model.ConfigKey]\n187: \texpected := sets.New[string]()\n188: \tif req.Forced {\n189: \t\t// Full update, expect everything\n190: \t\texpected.Merge(w.ResourceNames)\n191: \t} else {\n192: \t\t// The ambient store will send all of these as kind.AuthorizationPolicy, even if generated from PeerAuthentication,\n193: \t\t// so we can only fetch these ones.\n194: \t\tupdatedPolicies = model.ConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.AuthorizationPolicy)\n195: \n196: \t\tif len(updatedPolicies) == 0 {\n197: \t\t\t// This was a incremental push for a resource we don't watch... skip\n198: \t\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\n199: \t\t}\n200: \n201: \t\tfor k := range updatedPolicies {\n202: \t\t\texpected.Insert(k.Namespace + \"/\" + k.Name)\n203: \t\t}\n204: \t}\n205: \n206: \tresources := make(model.Resources, 0)\n207: \tpolicies := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(updatedPolicies)\n208: \tfor _, p := range policies {\n209: \t\tn := p.ResourceName()\n210: \t\texpected.Delete(n) // delete the generated policy name, left the removed ones\n211: \t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n212: \t\t\tName:     n,\n213: \t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(p.Authorization),\n214: \t\t})\n215: \t}\n216: \n217: \treturn resources, sets.SortedList(expected), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n218: }\n219: \n220: func (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n221: \tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\n222: \treturn resources, details, err\n223: }\n224: \n225: This is design to be highly optimized to delta updates,\n// and supports *on-demand* client usage. A client can subscribe with a wildcard subscription and get all\n// resources (with delta updates), or on-demand and only get responses for specifically subscribed resources.\n//\n// Incoming requests may be for VIP or Pod IP addresses. However, all responses are Workload resources, which are pod based.\n// This means subscribing to a VIP may end up pushing many resources of different name than the request.\n// On-demand clients are expected to handle this (for wildcard, this is not applicable, as they don't specify any resources at all).\nfunc (e WorkloadGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n\tproxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n\taddresses := req.AddressesUpdated\n\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n\tif !isReq && len(addresses) == 0 {\n\t\t// Nothing changed...\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif !w.Wildcard {\n\t\treturn e.generateDeltasOndemand(proxy, req, w)\n\t}\n\n\treqAddresses := addresses\n\tif isReq {\n\t\treqAddresses = nil\n\t}\n\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(reqAddresses)\n\t// Note: while \"removed\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\n\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\n\thave := sets.New[string]()\n\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\n\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n\t\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, nil, have, resources)\n\t}\n\n\tif isReq {\n\t\t// If it's a full push, AddressInformation won't have info to compute the full set of removals.\n\t\t// Instead, we need can see what resources are missing that we were subscribe to; those were removed.\n\t\t// During a request, a client will send a subscription request with its current state, so our removals will be:\n\t\t// ZtunnelCurrentResources - IstiodCurrentResources (+ empty `removed`).\n\t\tremoved = req.Delta.Subscribed.Difference(have).Merge(removed)\n\t}\n\n\t// Due to the high resource count in WDS at scale, we elide updating ResourceNames here.\n\t// A name will be ~50-100 bytes. So 50k pods would be 5MB per XDS client (plus overheads around GC, sets, fragmentation, etc).\n\t// Fortunately, we do not actually need this: the only time we need to know the state in Ztunnel is on reconnection which we handle from\n\t// `req.Delta.Subscribed`.\n\n\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n}\n\nfunc appendAddress(addr model.AddressInfo, requestedType string, aliases []string, have sets.Set[string], resources model.Resources) model.Resources {\n\tn := addr.ResourceName()\n\thave.Insert(n)\n\tswitch requestedType {\n\tcase v3.WorkloadType:\n\t\tif addr.GetWorkload() != nil {\n\t\t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n\t\t\t\tName:     n,\n\t\t\t\tAliases:  aliases,\n\t\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(addr.GetWorkload()), // TODO: pre-marshal\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\tcase v3.AddressType:\n\t\tproto := addr.Marshaled\n\t\tif proto == nil {\n\t\t\tproto = protoconv.MessageToAny(addr)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n\t\t\tName:     n,\n\t\t\tAliases:  aliases,\n\t\t\tResource: proto,\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\treturn resources\n}\n\nfunc (e WorkloadGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n\tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\n\treturn resources, details, err\n}\n\nfunc (e WorkloadGenerator) generateDeltasOndemand(\n\tproxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n\tisReq := req.IsRequest()\n\tsubs := w.ResourceNames\n\n\tvar addresses sets.String\n\tif isReq {\n\t\t// this is from request, we only send response for the subscribed address\n\t\t// At t0, a client request A, we only send A and additional resources back to the client.\n\t\t// At t1, a client request B, we only send B and additional resources back to the client, no A here.\n\t\taddresses = req.Delta.Subscribed\n\t} else {\n\t\t// this is from the external triggers instead of request\n\t\t// send response for all the subscribed intersect with the updated\n\t\taddresses = req.AddressesUpdated.Intersection(subs)\n\t}\n\n\t// We only need this for on-demand. This allows us to subscribe the client to resources they\n\t// didn't explicitly request.\n\t// For wildcard, they subscribe to everything already.\n\tadditional := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AdditionalPodSubscriptions(proxy, addresses, subs)\n\tif addresses == nil {\n\t\taddresses = additional\n\t} else {\n\t\taddresses.Merge(additional)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(addresses) == 0 {\n\t\tif isReq {\n\t\t\t// We need to respond for requests, even if we have nothing to respond with\n\t\t\treturn make(model.Resources, 0), nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n\t\t}\n\t\t// For NOP pushes, no need\n\t\treturn nil, nil, model.XdsLogDetails{}, false, nil\n\t}\n\taddrs, removed := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.AddressInformation(addresses)\n\t// Note: while \"removed\" is a weird name for a resource that never existed, this is how the spec works:\n\t// https://www.envoyproxy.io/docs/envoy/latest/api-docs/xds_protocol#id2\n\thave := sets.New[string]()\n\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0, len(addrs))\n\tfor _, addr := range addrs {\n\t\taliases := addr.Aliases()\n\t\tremoved.DeleteAll(aliases...)\n\t\tresources = appendAddress(addr, w.TypeUrl, aliases, have, resources)\n\t}\n\n\tproxy.Lock()\n\tdefer proxy.Unlock()\n\t// For on-demand, we may have requested a VIP but gotten Pod IPs back. We need to update\n\t// the internal book-keeping to subscribe to the Pods, so that we push updates to those Pods.\n\tw.ResourceNames = subs.Merge(have)\n\treturn resources, removed.UnsortedList(), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n}\n\ntype WorkloadRBACGenerator struct {\n\tServer *XDSServer\n}\n\nfunc (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) GenerateDeltas(\n\tproxy *model.Proxy,\n\treq *model.PushRequest,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n) (model.Resources, model.DeletedResources, model.XdsLogDetails, bool, error) {\n\tvar updatedPolicies sets.Set[model.ConfigKey]\n\texpected := sets.New[string]()\n\tif req.Forced {\n\t\t// Full update, expect everything\n\t\texpected.Merge(w.ResourceNames)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// The ambient store will send all of these as kind.AuthorizationPolicy, even if generated from PeerAuthentication,\n\t\t// so we can only fetch these ones.\n\t\tupdatedPolicies = model.ConfigsOfKind(req.ConfigsUpdated, kind.AuthorizationPolicy)\n\n\t\tif len(updatedPolicies) == 0 {\n\t\t\t// This was a incremental push for a resource we don't watch... skip\n\t\t\treturn nil, nil, model.DefaultXdsLogDetails, false, nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor k := range updatedPolicies {\n\t\t\texpected.Insert(k.Namespace + \"/\" + k.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresources := make(model.Resources, 0)\n\tpolicies := e.Server.Env.ServiceDiscovery.Policies(updatedPolicies)\n\tfor _, p := range policies {\n\t\tn := p.ResourceName()\n\t\texpected.Delete(n) // delete the generated policy name, left the removed ones\n\t\tresources = append(resources, &discovery.Resource{\n\t\t\tName:     n,\n\t\t\tResource: protoconv.MessageToAny(p.Authorization),\n\t\t})\n\t}\n\n\treturn resources, sets.SortedList(expected), model.XdsLogDetails{}, true, nil\n}\n\nfunc (e WorkloadRBACGenerator) Generate(proxy *model.Proxy, w *model.WatchedResource, req *model.PushRequest) (model.Resources, model.XdsLogDetails, error) {\n\tresources, _, details, _, err := e.GenerateDeltas(proxy, req, w)\n\treturn resources, details, err\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ model.XdsResourceGenerator      = &WorkloadRBACGenerator{}\n\t_ model.XdsDeltaResourceGenerator = &WorkloadRBACGenerator{}\n)\nWORKLOAD_EOF\n\n# Create deltatest.go\ncat << 'DELTATEST_EOF' > pilot/pkg/xds/deltatest.go\n// Copyright Istio Authors\n//\n// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\n// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\n// You may obtain a copy of the License at\n//\n//     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n//\n// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\n// distributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\n// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and\n// limitations under the License.\n\npackage xds\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\n\tdiscovery \"github.com/envoyproxy/go-control-plane/envoy/service/discovery/v3\"\n\t\"github.com/google/go-cmp/cmp\"\n\t\"google.golang.org/protobuf/testing/protocmp\"\n\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/model\"\n\tv3 \"istio.io/istio/pilot/pkg/xds/v3\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/slices\"\n\t\"istio.io/istio/pkg/util/sets\"\n)\n\nvar knownOptimizationGaps = sets.New(\n\t\"BlackHoleCluster\",\n\t\"InboundPassthroughCluster\",\n\t\"PassthroughCluster\",\n)\n\n// compareDiff compares a Delta and SotW XDS response. This allows checking that the Delta XDS\n// response returned the optimal result. Checks include correctness checks (e.g. if a config changed,\n// we must include it) and possible optimizations (e.g. we sent a config, but it was not changed).\nfunc (s *XDSServer) compareDiff(\n\tcon *Connection,\n\tw *model.WatchedResource,\n\tsotwRes model.Resources,\n\tdeltaRes model.Resources,\n\tdeleted model.DeletedResources,\n\tusedDelta bool,\n\tdelta model.ResourceDelta,\n\tincremental bool,\n) {\n\tcurrent := con.proxy.GetWatchedResource(w.TypeUrl).LastResources\n\tif current == nil {\n\t\tlog.Debugf(\"ADS:%s: resources initialized\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl))\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tif deltaRes == nil && deleted == nil && len(sotwRes) == 0 {\n\t\t// TODO: it suspicious full is never nil - are there case where we should be deleting everything?\n\t\t// Both SotW and Delta did not respond, nothing to compare\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\tnewByName := slices.GroupUnique(sotwRes, (*discovery.Resource).GetName)\n\tcurByName := slices.GroupUnique(current, (*discovery.Resource).GetName)\n\n\twatched := w.ResourceNames\n\n\tdetails := fmt.Sprintf(\"last:%v sotw:%v delta:%v-%v\", len(current), len(sotwRes), len(deltaRes), len(deleted))\n\twantDeleted := sets.New[string]()\n\twantChanged := sets.New[string]()\n\twantUnchanged := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, c := range current {\n\t\tn := newByName[c.Name]\n\t\tif n == nil {\n\t\t\t// We had a resource, but SotW didn't generate it.\n\t\t\tif watched.Contains(c.Name) {\n\t\t\t\t// We only need to delete it if Envoy is watching. Otherwise, it may have simply unsubscribed\n\t\t\t\twantDeleted.Insert(c.Name)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if diff := cmp.Diff(c.Resource, n.Resource, protocmp.Transform()); diff != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Resource was modified\n\t\t\twantChanged.Insert(c.Name)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// No diff. Ideally delta doesn't send any update here\n\t\t\twantUnchanged.Insert(c.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tfor _, v := range sotwRes {\n\t\tif _, f := curByName[v.Name]; !f {\n\t\t\t// Resource is added. Delta doesn't distinguish add vs update, so just put it with changed\n\t\t\twantChanged.Insert(v.Name)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tgotDeleted := sets.New[string]()\n\tif usedDelta {\n\t\tgotDeleted.InsertAll(deleted...)\n\t}\n\tgotChanged := sets.New[string]()\n\tfor _, v := range deltaRes {\n\t\tgotChanged.Insert(v.Name)\n\t}\n\n\t// BUGS\n\textraDeletes := sets.SortedList(gotDeleted.Difference(wantDeleted))\n\tmissedDeletes := sets.SortedList(wantDeleted.Difference(gotDeleted))\n\tmissedChanges := sets.SortedList(wantChanged.Difference(gotChanged))\n\n\t// Optimization Potential\n\textraChanges := sets.SortedList(gotChanged.Difference(wantChanged).Difference(knownOptimizationGaps))\n\tif len(delta.Subscribed) > 0 {\n\t\t// Delta is configured to build only the request resources. Make sense we didn't build anything extra\n\t\tif !wantChanged.SupersetOf(gotChanged) {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s unexpected resources: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, wantChanged.Difference(gotChanged))\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Still make sure we didn't delete anything extra\n\t\tif len(extraDeletes) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s unexpected deletions: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, extraDeletes)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tif len(extraDeletes) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s unexpected deletions: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, extraDeletes)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(missedDeletes) > 0 && !incremental {\n\t\t\t// We can skip this if we are incremental; this expects us to only send a partial list. So these are not actually deletes\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed deletions: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, missedDeletes)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(missedChanges) > 0 {\n\t\t\tlog.Errorf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed changes: %v %v\", v3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, details, missedChanges)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif len(extraChanges) > 0 {\n\t\t\tif usedDelta {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Infof(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed possible optimization: %v. deleted:%v changed:%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, extraChanges, len(gotDeleted), len(gotChanged))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Debugf(\"%s: TEST for node:%s missed possible optimization: %v. deleted:%v changed:%v\",\n\t\t\t\t\tv3.GetShortType(w.TypeUrl), con.proxy.ID, extraChanges, len(gotDeleted), len(gotChanged))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc applyDelta(message model.Resources, resp *discovery.DeltaDiscoveryResponse) model.Resources {\n\tdeleted := sets.New(resp.RemovedResources...)\n\tbyName := map[string]*discovery.Resource{}\n\tfor _, v := range resp.Resources {\n\t\tbyName[v.Name] = v\n\t}\n\tres := model.Resources{}\n\tfor _, m := range message {\n\t\tif deleted.Contains(m.Name) {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tif replaced := byName[m.Name]; replaced != nil {\n\t\t\tres = append(res, replaced)\n\t\t\tdelete(byName, m.Name)\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\tres = append(res, m)\n\t}\n\tfor _, v := range byName {\n\t\tres = append(res, v)\n\t}\n\treturn res\n}\nDELTATEST_EOF\n\necho \"Created workload.go and deltatest.go\"\n","description":"Create workload.go and deltatest.go with XDSServer naming"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7135,"cache_read_input_tokens":133337,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":7135},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"731dac43-d059-4b49-b6ef-e931483c341b","uuid":"35abdeb4-2e95-485e-b03d-2067475204f4"}
